"{\"commits\":[{\"author\":\"Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>\",\"commit\":\"dec661c\",\"date\":\"2025-09-25\",\"message\":\"wolfssl: fix error check in shutdown\\n\\nWhen trying to send the TLS shutdown, use the return code\\nto check for the cause.\\n\\nReported in Joshua's sarif data\\n\\nCloses #18729\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"wolfssl: fix error check in shutdown\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/dec661c81cb0bc55e089b67112ab995eeba350eb\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"d77a23c\",\"date\":\"2025-08-26\",\"message\":\"tests: remove the QUIT filters\\n\\nOnce added in cd4aee156f64f44bb1f1 to work around connection shutdown\\nissues.\\n\\nCloses #18405\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"tests: remove the QUIT filters\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/d77a23c263f108e6cb347d2e756a79cf53934275\"},{\"author\":\"Stanislav Fort <stanislav.fort@aisle.com>\",\"commit\":\"b0aba10\",\"date\":\"2025-11-10\",\"message\":\"cshutdn: acknowledge FD_SETSIZE for shutdown descriptors\\n\\nIn the logic called for curl_multi_fdset().\\n\\nFile descriptors larger than FD_SETSIZE size are simply ignored, which\\nof course will make things break but at least it does not trash memory.\\n\\nReported-by: Stanislav Fort (Aisle Research)\\nCloses #19439\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"cshutdn: acknowledge FD_SETSIZE for shutdown descriptors\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/b0aba1005bf4f0f1eef467cf1e0ef4728421a6fd\"},{\"author\":\"Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>\",\"commit\":\"80ac5fb\",\"date\":\"2025-09-10\",\"message\":\"easy_getinfo: check magic, Curl_close safety\\n\\nCheck the easy handles magic in calls to curl_easy_getinfo().\\nIn Curl_close() clear the magic after DNS shutdown since we'd\\nlike to see tracing for this.\\nWhen clearing the magic, also clear the verbose flag so we\\nno longer call DEBUGFUNCTION on such a handle.\\n\\nCloses #18511\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"easy_getinfo: check magic, Curl_close safety\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/80ac5fb2ec29203fd9d87d705f72e8cd2afee501\"},{\"author\":\"Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>\",\"commit\":\"8ce16e7\",\"date\":\"2026-01-19\",\"message\":\"timeout handling: auto-detect effective timeout\\n\\nWhen checking a transfer for being expired via `Curl_timeleft_ms()`,\\neleminate the `bool connecting` parameter and have the function check\\nthe `mstate` of the transfer instead.\\n\\nAdvantages:\\n* eleminate the caller needing awareness if the transfer is\\n  connecting or in a later state\\n* fix pingpong timeout handling to check the correct timeout\\n  during \\\"proto_connect\\\" phases\\n* avoid using \\\"connecting\\\" timeouts during establishing a secondary\\n  connection (e.g. FTP) since this would use the timestamp from\\n  the original, primary connect and thus be... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"timeout handling: auto-detect effective timeout\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/8ce16e7bf29925eb89b602861623695efc7a4af6\"},{\"author\":\"Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>\",\"commit\":\"1a57302\",\"date\":\"2026-01-15\",\"message\":\"ratelimit: download finetune\\n\\nWhen a download size is known and rate limiting is in effect, adjust the\\nduration of each measurement step and its rate for maximum precision.\\n\\nSince it is unpredictable how long the last bytes of a download will\\ntake, download speed can be thrown off if the \\\"last bytes\\\" are a\\nsignificant amount of the total download. Make the \\\"last bytes\\\" small in\\ncomparision to the rest and \\\"stretch\\\" the rate limit intervals to\\naccommodate the difference.\\n\\nFix ngtcp2 receive data acknowldgements to be based on a local window\\nsize tracking. This allows window updates controlled b... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"ratelimit: download finetune\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/1a57302d1acaab3432d31435f1f42f6290594304\"},{\"author\":\"Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>\",\"commit\":\"2de22a0\",\"date\":\"2025-12-15\",\"message\":\"lib: keep timestamp in easy handle\\n\\nUse `data->progress.now` as the timestamp of proecssing a transfer.\\nUpdate it on significant events and refrain from calling `curlx_now()`\\nin many places.\\n\\nThe problem this addresses is\\na) calling curlx_now() has costs, depending on platform. Calling it\\n   every time results in 25% increase `./runtest` duration on macOS.\\nb) we used to pass a `struct curltime *` around to save on calls, but\\n   when some method directly use `curx_now()` and some use the passed\\n   pointer, the transfer experienes non-linear time. This results in\\n   timeline checks to report eve... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"lib: keep timestamp in easy handle\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/2de22a00c7adb81b4e5cbc90785e29b4b083c1ed\"},{\"author\":\"Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>\",\"commit\":\"f6e8531\",\"date\":\"2026-01-07\",\"message\":\"http/3: add description for known server error codes\\n\\nWhen a server resets a stream with an error code, list that code\\nand its known name in the failure message of the transfer.\\n\\nRef: #20195\\nCloses #20202\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"http/3: add description for known server error codes\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/f6e8531c03edcef9da222d1794d52d14247cd5ce\"},{\"author\":\"Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>\",\"commit\":\"891566c\",\"date\":\"2025-12-05\",\"message\":\"ftp: make EPRT connections non-blocking\\n\\nOn platforms where neither accept4 nor fcntl was available, an\\nEPRT connection did not send the accepted socket as non-blocking.\\n\\nThis became apparent when TLS was in use and the test receive\\non shutdown did simply hang.\\n\\nReported-by: Denis Goleshchikhin\\nFixes #19753\\nCloses #19851\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"ftp: make EPRT connections non-blocking\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/891566c72da47250c13047f7df0d8aa94a6ff842\"},{\"author\":\"Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>\",\"commit\":\"16b44f6\",\"date\":\"2025-11-19\",\"message\":\"multi: simplify admin handle processing\\n\\nFold the special connection pool shutdown handling in multi the things\\nthe admin handle cares about. Add the admin handle to the 'process'\\nbitset, deduce it from the 'running' count.\\n\\nThe admin handle is the processed like any other transfer, but has a\\nspecial case in `multi_runsingle()`. Simplifies all other multi\\nprocessing parts.\\n\\nCloses #19604\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"multi: simplify admin handle processing\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/16b44f6a3ad13386a25dfc005794696a69f949b0\"},{\"author\":\"Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>\",\"commit\":\"1e85cb4\",\"date\":\"2025-10-31\",\"message\":\"scp/sftp: fix disconnect\\n\\nWhen a SCP/SFTP connection calls the protocol handler disconnect, it\\nrequired the connections *and* the easy handles SSH meta data to be\\npresent. When the disconnect is called with an admin handle, the easy\\nmeta data is not present, which prevented the shutdown to run.\\n\\nThe easy meta data is however not necessary to run the shutdown state\\nmachine. Calling it with a NULL `sshp` is fine. To avoid any mixups,\\ncheck `sshp` in state operations that need it.\\n\\nFixes #19293\\nReported-by: And-yW on github\\nCloses #19295\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"scp/sftp: fix disconnect\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/1e85cb4b7b88c2b2a21ff425eee071128106b085\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"434e531\",\"date\":\"2026-02-11\",\"message\":\"tool_operate: split post_per_transfer into sub functions\\n\\nTo make it easier to read and manage. Reduce complexity.\\n\\nCloses #20560\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"tool_operate: split post_per_transfer into sub functions\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/434e5312f45c8a3bc06f26ec0b2820131e60583a\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"236c7d3\",\"date\":\"2026-01-13\",\"message\":\"curl_easy_nextheader.md: a new transfer invalidates 'prev'\\n\\nCloses #20285\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"curl_easy_nextheader.md: a new transfer invalidates 'prev'\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/236c7d3c4d115795f87eb58aff3958bfb81e951e\"},{\"author\":\"Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>\",\"commit\":\"68b94da\",\"date\":\"2026-01-02\",\"message\":\"pytest: test 16_01 stabilize\\n\\nWhen checking the reported times of a transfer, do not exptect\\nthe 'queue' time to be in any relation to others. 'queue' uses its own\\nstart timestamp and the reported duration is thereofore independant.\\n\\nRef: #20112\\nCloses #20163\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"pytest: test 16_01 stabilize\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/68b94daeb89399caec06c4a614805f5ab6974b41\"},{\"author\":\"Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>\",\"commit\":\"52ac810\",\"date\":\"2025-12-29\",\"message\":\"ratelimit blocking: fix busy loop\\n\\nFix the pollset in perform state to not add sockets for directions\\nthat are blocked. This otherwise will lead to busy loops for a\\ntransfer that cannot be progressed.\\n\\nReported-by: Fizn-Ahmd on github\\nFixes #20091\\nCloses #20109\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"ratelimit blocking: fix busy loop\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/52ac8104e1396998110e0760ac780f4f2414831f\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"71d5525\",\"date\":\"2025-10-16\",\"message\":\"connect: remove redundant condition in shutdown start\\n\\nPointed out by CodeSonar\\n\\nCloses #19079\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"connect: remove redundant condition in shutdown start\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/71d5525113171903deb9b97cc1913c30f515cc63\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"142d61a\",\"date\":\"2025-10-10\",\"message\":\"doswin: CloseHandle the thread on shutdown\\n\\nAs this is in the tool shutdown the impact of it was nothing.\\n\\nAlso, move two global variables to local.\\n\\nFollow-up to 9a2663322c330ff11275abafd612e9c\\n\\nReported-by: Joshua Rogers\\nCloses #18996\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"doswin: CloseHandle the thread on shutdown\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/142d61a0ee71b2c58f8f03495fb43d83b478db64\"},{\"author\":\"Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>\",\"commit\":\"88fc6c4\",\"date\":\"2025-08-20\",\"message\":\"threaded-resolver: fix shutdown\\n\\nChanged strategy to start up and terminate resolver thread.\\n\\nWhen starting up:\\n\\nStart the thread with mutex acquired, wait for signal from thread that\\nit started and has incremented the ref counter. Thread set\\npthread_cancel() to disabled before that and only enables cancelling\\nduring resolving itself. This assure that the ref counter is correct and\\nthe unlinking of the resolve context always happens.\\n\\nWhen shutting down resolving:\\n\\nIf ref counting shows thread has finished, join it, free everything. If\\nthread has not finished, try pthread_cancel() (non Windows... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"threaded-resolver: fix shutdown\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/88fc6c491f043ed184ea2cf1a17b651427fbbbf5\"},{\"author\":\"Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>\",\"commit\":\"c1372df\",\"date\":\"2025-08-07\",\"message\":\"ares: destroy channel on shutdown\\n\\nWhen we cancel async resolv operations, we have kept an existing ares\\nchannel open. This seems unreliable as reported in #18216.\\n\\nTo get reliable behaviour, always destroy the ares channel on async\\nshutdown and create a new one on demand.\\n\\nFixes #18216\\nReported-by: devgs on github\\nCloses #18217\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"ares: destroy channel on shutdown\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/c1372df2e2fa93d4435aeb50d068643b53194000\"},{\"author\":\"Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>\",\"commit\":\"9703dab\",\"date\":\"2026-01-15\",\"message\":\"lib: make sigpipe handling more lazy\\n\\nDefine `struct Curl_sigpipe_ctx` that can be passed as argunent\\nto \\\"lower\\\" functions so that applying a transfers 'no_signal'\\nsetting can be delayed as much as possible and sometimes avoided\\nalltogether.\\n\\nFixes #20326\\nCloses #20329\\nReported-by: Dag Haavi Finstad\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"lib: make sigpipe handling more lazy\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/9703dabd77b5fbfda26944462afcc84242eaa486\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"18af4e4\",\"date\":\"2025-12-25\",\"message\":\"cookie: flush better\\n\\nThe cookie flushing (saving to a cookie jar) should only be done if a\\ntransfer has been started. This is now done by checking the\\ncookies->running field, which is not reset in curl_easy_reset() so the\\nsaving works correctly even after a call to that.\\n\\nFollow-up to fd6eb8d6e77d95e71c\\n\\nVerified by test 1920\\n\\nReported-by: Alexander Batischev\\nFixes #20090\\nCloses #20094\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"cookie: flush better\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/18af4e4e109f3825d1891c32aacb064fdd7e9dc4\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"a3f866a\",\"date\":\"2025-12-21\",\"message\":\"transfer: drop unused `vquic/vquic.h` include\\n\\nCloses #20059\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"transfer: drop unused `vquic/vquic.h` include\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/a3f866ac76fab51b3ec76d6bcc92629c5aaa1f30\"},{\"author\":\"Christian Schmitz <support@monkeybreadsoftware.de>\",\"commit\":\"624d98e\",\"date\":\"2025-12-11\",\"message\":\"transfer: remove @param for err for xfer_recv_resp function.\\n\\nThe err parameter in xfer_recv_resp doesn't exist anymore. Removed in\\ncb2bcb681fc9044d2a7adaaa621.\\n\\nCloses #19937\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"transfer: remove @param for err for xfer_recv_resp function.\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/624d98e79ccde1304f40967509b56c32feb2da48\"},{\"author\":\"Xiaoke Wang <xkernel.wang@foxmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"e2a12fc\",\"date\":\"2025-11-01\",\"message\":\"vtls: properly handle SSL shutdown timeout\\n\\nCloses #19323\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"vtls: properly handle SSL shutdown timeout\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/e2a12fcbdbe9fa515e0389995318f02f898a4b0b\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"e7a5184\",\"date\":\"2025-10-06\",\"message\":\"openssl: call SSL_get_error() with proper error\\n\\nThe error function should be called with the return code from the\\nprevious call to SSL_shutdown() as argument.\\n\\nCloses #18872\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"openssl: call SSL_get_error() with proper error\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/e7a5184fa1ebcd68ead0e3e9b78340296acac350\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"c9afcec\",\"date\":\"2025-02-04\",\"message\":\"transfer: fix returning init failures from `xfer_recv_shutdown_started()`\\n\\nBefore this patch it returned `CURLE_FAILED_INIT` on init failures, with\\nthe value of 2. Fix it to return `false`.\\n\\nSeen with clang 18.1.8:\\n```\\n../lib/transfer.c(181,12): warning: integer constant not in range of enumerated type 'bool' [-Wassign-enum]\\n  181 |     return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\\n      |            ^\\n../lib/transfer.c(181,12): warning: implicit conversion from enumeration type 'CURLcode' to different enumeration type 'bool' [-Wenum-conversion]\\n  181 |     return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\\n      |     ~~~~~~ ^~~~~~~~~~~... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"transfer: fix returning init failures from `xfer_recv_shutdown_started()`\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/c9afcecee94284e754d82354c79e4e37f09bff61\"},{\"author\":\"Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>\",\"commit\":\"faa2db2\",\"date\":\"2025-08-19\",\"message\":\"ares: handle channel being destroyed early\\n\\nWe are destroying the ares channel already when we shutdown\\nresolving. Querying the pollset afterwards is still happening,\\nespecially in event based processing and needs to work in the\\nabsence of a channel.\\n\\nFixes #18317\\nReported-by: Natris on github\\nCloses #18318\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"ares: handle channel being destroyed early\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/faa2db202b9b90a157f1204d1e028b377042841b\"},{\"author\":\"Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>\",\"commit\":\"f062b4b\",\"date\":\"2026-01-28\",\"message\":\"pytest: remove 03_02\\n\\nThe test for restarting the server during ongoing transfers does not\\nwork reliably for HTTP/3. This seems due to the nature of UDP/QUIC where\\nthe client may learn about a closed connection at any time, not only\\nwhen starting a new request.\\n\\nRemove the test.\\n\\nCloses #20458\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"pytest: remove 03_02\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/f062b4b903547e26b336f73c5103c9521382f302\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"74f7505\",\"date\":\"2025-11-23\",\"message\":\"asyn-ares: remove hostname free on OOM\\n\\nThe freeing of the already allocated hostname is done by\\nCurl_async_shutdown(). This extra free in the RR code path made a\\ndouble-free.\\n\\nPresumably not detected because the CI torture tests don't run HTTPS-RR\\nenabled?\\n\\nFollow-up to 8d0bfe74fb\\nCloses #19658\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"asyn-ares: remove hostname free on OOM\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/74f7505974836f633c955dbce7514f12f78ddc5b\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"df6f3ae\",\"date\":\"2026-02-12\",\"message\":\"multi: split multi_runsingle into sub functions\\n\\nTo reduce complexity.\\n\\n- is_finished() checks if the individual transfer is done\\n\\n- handle_completed() is the logic that runs for a completed\\n  transfer\\n\\nCloses #20573\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"multi: split multi_runsingle into sub functions\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/df6f3ae60ab915e0993d435f416ac763c5095255\"},{\"author\":\"Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>\",\"commit\":\"0f7b182\",\"date\":\"2025-12-19\",\"message\":\"ngtcp2: retune window sizes\\n\\nWith 24b36fd stream flow control window sizes have been set too\\nrestrictive, crippling transfer rates when no rate limit is in effect.\\n\\nDisable ngtcp3 stream window auto-tuning and extend the stream window\\nfrom the small initial size to the effective rate limit. If no rate\\nlimit is configured, extend stream window to maximum value right away.\\n\\nThis cannot shrink the stream window later, however. But growing the\\nlimit or removing it, will work mid download.\\n\\nFixes #20030\\nReported-by: koujaz on github\\nCloses #20033\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"ngtcp2: retune window sizes\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/0f7b182f2f13146b7147991497db4632cec38e92\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"40fee50\",\"date\":\"2026-01-11\",\"message\":\"tool_operate: split single_transfer into sub functions\\n\\nTo make it more manageable and less complex\\n\\nCloses #20258\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"tool_operate: split single_transfer into sub functions\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/40fee505a03b357a7a9869317437fff9e16d8ba7\"}],\"limitHit\":true,\"query\":\"type:commit (message:transfer OR message:shutdown OR message:xfer_recv) repo:github.com/curl/curl after:2024-12-01 count:20\",\"totalCount\":32}"
58. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_commit_search @ -
Input
{
  "after": "2024-12-01",
  "count": 20,
  "messageTerms": [
    "cookie",
    "domain",
    "security"
  ],
  "repos": [
    "github.com/curl/curl"
  ]
}
Output
"{\"commits\":[{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"18af4e4\",\"date\":\"2025-12-25\",\"message\":\"cookie: flush better\\n\\nThe cookie flushing (saving to a cookie jar) should only be done if a\\ntransfer has been started. This is now done by checking the\\ncookies->running field, which is not reset in curl_easy_reset() so the\\nsaving works correctly even after a call to that.\\n\\nFollow-up to fd6eb8d6e77d95e71c\\n\\nVerified by test 1920\\n\\nReported-by: Alexander Batischev\\nFixes #20090\\nCloses #20094\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"cookie: flush better\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/18af4e4e109f3825d1891c32aacb064fdd7e9dc4\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"87cbeec\",\"date\":\"2025-09-07\",\"message\":\"windows: stop passing unused, optional argument for Win9x compatibility\\n\\nExpiry timestamp in `AcquireCredentialsHandle()` (SSPI) and\\n`InitializeSecurityContext()` (Schannel) calls. The argument is optional\\nin both. The returned value was never used in curl. The reason for\\npassing it was Windows 95 compatibility, according to comments in\\nthe SSPI code. curl no longer supports Windows 95.\\n\\nRef: https://learn.microsoft.com/windows/win32/api/sspi/nf-sspi-acquirecredentialshandlea\\nRef: https://learn.microsoft.com/windows/win32/secauthn/initializesecuritycontext--schannel\\n\\nRef: 3fe531196771c8e81f917... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"windows: stop passing unused, optional argument for Win9x compatibility\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/87cbeecee4118fa3dcc8a0179b1c3b43e4775cb4\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"cc50f05\",\"date\":\"2025-09-11\",\"message\":\"GHA/codeql: re-enable for C with the default query pack\\n\\nEarlier we used `security-extended` and tried `security-and-quality`.\\nTry the default to see how it works.\\n\\nCodeQL no longer uses the project's Actions cache, also fixing\\nthe previously seen repeat cache entry issue.\\n\\n- switch to `manual` build. It's 3x faster than the default `autobuild`.\\n- enable more dependencies to increase coverage.\\n- docs/tests/CI.md: re-add CodeQL.\\n\\nRef: https://docs.github.com/en/code-security/code-scanning/managing-your-code-scanning-configuration/codeql-query-suites\\nRef: https://docs.github.com/en/code-security... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"GHA/codeql: re-enable for C with the default query pack\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/cc50f05370981e4933504e8aaec6b15880ff847f\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"524936f\",\"date\":\"2025-12-07\",\"message\":\"cookie: when parsing a cookie header, delay all allocations until okay\\n\\nTo avoid wasting time allocating data for incoming cookies that are\\ndiscarded for one reason or another, delay allocations until after\\nverifications are done.\\n\\nCloses #19864\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"cookie: when parsing a cookie header, delay all allocations until okay\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/524936fbebf2a37f5d02c97da2842fa6655a25bd\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"c6e5dfa\",\"date\":\"2025-12-07\",\"message\":\"cookie: allocate the main struct once cookie is fine\\n\\nThis delays the allocating of the cookie struct until after all the\\nchecks have been done, as many cookies are received and discarded\\ninstead of accepted and this then saves one allocation for every\\ndiscarded cookie.\\n\\nCloses #19864\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"cookie: allocate the main struct once cookie is fine\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/c6e5dfa2c67570f98225959148a6e78c1e2180f2\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"e9ababe\",\"date\":\"2025-10-06\",\"message\":\"windows: use native error code types more\\n\\n- curlx_get_winapi_error: accept DWORD (was: int), move casts one level\\n  up the callstack.\\n\\n- sspi: bump some types to `SECURITY_STATUS` (int -> LONG).\\n\\n- digest_sspi: drop unnecessary cast.\\n\\nCloses #18868\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"windows: use native error code types more\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/e9ababe9aa4c1f7d727494d660650af934db523b\"},{\"author\":\"Arnav-Purushotam-CUBoulder <arpu9852@colorado.edu>\",\"commit\":\"9b20a67\",\"date\":\"2026-01-24\",\"message\":\"include: mask computed auth/proto bitmasks to 32 bits\\n\\nGCC 15.2 warns when assigning computed \\\"all\\\" bitmask macros to 32-bit\\nflag types because negated masks expand to the full width of unsigned\\nlong/long on 64-bit platforms.\\n\\nMask these macros to a 32-bit domain so they do not set high bits and\\navoid -Woverflow/-Wconversion warnings in callers.\\n\\nReported-by: Patrick Monnerat\\nFixes #20242\\nCloses #20416\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"include: mask computed auth/proto bitmasks to 32 bits\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/9b20a672b865fd69d7596ca7dcdfc0cbcf6b9dc7\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"3e1179a\",\"date\":\"2026-01-09\",\"message\":\"KNOWN_BUGS.md: absolute Unix domain filename for SOCKS on Windows\\n\\nCloses #20236\\nCloses #19825\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"KNOWN_BUGS.md: absolute Unix domain filename for SOCKS on Windows\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/3e1179a695528fa31161d348e5ca68b1ab3845e1\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"b5ffe30\",\"date\":\"2025-09-26\",\"message\":\"cf-ip-happy: mention unix domain path, not port number\\n\\nIn the connect error message if a unix domain socket was used.\\n\\nReported-by: kuchara on github\\nRef: #18748\\nCloses #18749\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"cf-ip-happy: mention unix domain path, not port number\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/b5ffe30e5b7e79d51ec96c266538eb5586c6a0dd\"},{\"author\":\"pojomi <potter.jonathan.m@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"dc739fe\",\"date\":\"2026-01-04\",\"message\":\"config2setopts: add space in cookie header with multiple -b\\n\\nCloses #20184\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"config2setopts: add space in cookie header with multiple -b\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/dc739fee166d3012e612d7a1fb2952840dd252b1\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"a78a07d\",\"date\":\"2025-12-07\",\"message\":\"cookie: cleanups and improvements\\n\\n- Stricter cookie validation with earlier rejection of empty/invalid\\n  cookie names\\n\\n- secure and httponly attributes no longer accept = with empty values\\n  (only bare keywords)\\n\\n- Validation checks (length, TAB, prefixes) moved into the first\\n  name/value pair block for better code organization\\n\\n- Deferred time(NULL) calls for better performance when expires/max-age\\n  aren't used\\n\\n- Simplified loop control flow by removing done flag\\n\\n- The cookie size restriction now only applies to name + value, not other\\n  parts of the header line.\\n\\n- Fixed a gcc 4.8.1 qui... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"cookie: cleanups and improvements\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/a78a07d3a9dc808a51f464d0dd297939e544e2a4\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"51587f6\",\"date\":\"2025-12-05\",\"message\":\"mdlinkcheck: detect and check \\\"raw\\\" links\\n\\n- URLs specified outside of the markdown []() are now extracted and\\n  checked\\n\\n- also check TODO, FAQ and KNOWN_BUGS\\n\\n- more aggressive avoiding to check github.com/curl/curl, all uses of\\n  example domains and some more established URLs on the curl.se site\\n\\n- list all errors in the end to make them easier to spot in CI logs\\n\\nCloses #19848\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"mdlinkcheck: detect and check \\\"raw\\\" links\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/51587f6f14af22e932d2448ed3a7b0052e880ea1\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"e0443a7\",\"date\":\"2025-02-05\",\"message\":\"cmake: lib order fixes for picky linkers (e.g. binutils `ld`)\\n\\nThis issue was not addressed with CMake builds so far. curl-for-win\\nworked thanks to its `-Wl,--start-group` workaround. It affects\\nbinutils `ld` linking statically. Shared linking and llvm's `lld`\\ndoesn't need strict lib order, and are not affected.\\n\\nThe solution is to pass libs in dependency order, with least dependent\\n(e.g. system) libs last. In case of cyclic dependency, may pass libs\\ntwice.\\n\\nFix most issues by moving Windows system libs `ws2_32` and `bcrypt`\\nlast, and move SSH libs first due to their dependence on crypto\\nbacke... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"cmake: lib order fixes for picky linkers (e.g. binutils `ld`)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/e0443a76c4fdd7f3b0dc413f1f7f28171b359e15\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"e8a4068\",\"date\":\"2025-12-04\",\"message\":\"docs: clarify how to do unix domain sockets with SOCKS proxy\\n\\nRef: #19825\\nCloses #19829\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"docs: clarify how to do unix domain sockets with SOCKS proxy\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/e8a4068e6868424d83a3394da4810c93a43c070a\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"a093c93\",\"date\":\"2025-12-07\",\"message\":\"cookie: only keep and use the canonical cleaned up path\\n\\nInstead of keeping both versions around.\\n\\nCloses #19864\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"cookie: only keep and use the canonical cleaned up path\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/a093c93994d2afed3beed94809844c961d3f4a64\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"eaa7651\",\"date\":\"2025-11-17\",\"message\":\"lib: replace `_tcsncpy`/`wcsncpy`/`wcscpy` with `_s` counterparts (Windows)\\n\\nReplace:\\n- curl_sspi: macro `_tcsncpy()` with `_tcsncpy_s()`.\\n- curlx/fopen: `wcsncpy()` with `wcsncpy_s()`.\\n- curlx/fopen: `wcscpy()` with `wcscpy_s()`.\\n\\nUse of the pre-existing functions were safe. This patch aims to use the\\nrecommended Windows CRT functions. Handle errors returned by them. Also\\nto avoid the compiler warnings silenced via `_CRT_SECURE_NO_WARNINGS`:\\n\\n```\\nlib/curl_sspi.c(152): warning C4996: 'wcsncpy': This function or variable may be unsafe. Consider using wcsncpy_s instead. To disable deprecation, u... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"lib: replace `_tcsncpy`/`wcsncpy`/`wcscpy` with `_s` counterparts (Windows)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/eaa7651374898cef89e5783b0219bdffcc0eb60e\"},{\"author\":\"Patrick Monnerat <patrick@monnerat.net>\",\"commit\":\"67c75b6\",\"date\":\"2025-10-10\",\"message\":\"os400: document threads handling in code.\\n\\nThis is to clarify threads unavaibility check and handling for security\\nbug busters unaware of OS400 specificities.\\n\\nFixes #18967\\nCloses #19009\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"os400: document threads handling in code.\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/67c75b67125d09029288dd490050471c46c50ece\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"f97e62f\",\"date\":\"2025-12-02\",\"message\":\"sws: fix binding to unix socket on Windows\\n\\nWindows 10.17063+ (having unix socket support) fails to set for unix\\nsockets the `SO_REUSEADDR` option, with error 10045 (`WSAEOPNOTSUPP`),\\nand also fails to set `SO_KEEPALIVE` with error 10042 (`WSAENOPROTOOPT`).\\n\\nFix by not enabling these socket options on Windows for unix sockets.\\n\\nAlso:\\n- fixing test 1435, 1436 to run in CI.\\n- fixing the `socksd` test server for test 1467, 1468, 1470. But, also\\n  disable these for now due to another Windows issue: #19825\\n\\nRef: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/68791319/unix-domain-socket-bind-failed-in-windows/... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"sws: fix binding to unix socket on Windows\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/f97e62ff147feff09e1a29a2db6b4a7f928d75a0\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"51b85bd\",\"date\":\"2025-10-06\",\"message\":\"windows: use consistent format when showing error codes\\n\\nFor `GetLastError()` and `SECURITY_STATUS`:\\n0x-prefixed, 8-digit, lowercase, hex: 0x1234abcd\\n\\nAlso: say `GetLastError()` instead of `errno` in one message.\\n\\nCloses #18877\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"windows: use consistent format when showing error codes\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/51b85bdc6cd48a55c75b03afcf1f057839564ae8\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"ae1597c\",\"date\":\"2026-01-02\",\"message\":\"VULN-DISCLOSURE-POLICY.md: CRLF in data\\n\\nwe reject the idea of *CRLF injection* by the user itself as a general\\nsecurity problem\\n\\nCloses #20157\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"VULN-DISCLOSURE-POLICY.md: CRLF in data\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/ae1597c312d7614ac7a8be627fff0961c8e26e3c\"},{\"author\":\"Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>\",\"commit\":\"692c7f1\",\"date\":\"2025-10-27\",\"message\":\"TLS: IP address verification, extend test\\n\\nChange the test certificate to carry a altname 'dns:127.0.0.1' which\\nshould *not* match in test_17_05_bad_ip_addr.\\n\\nwolfSSL: since `wolfSSL_check_domain_name()` does not differentiate\\nbetween DNS and IP names, use if only for DNS names. For IP addresses,\\nget the peer certificate after the handshake and check that using\\nwolfSSL_X509_check_ip_asc().\\n\\nUnfortunately, this succeeds where it should not, as wolfSSL internally\\nused the same check code for both cases. So, skip the test case until\\nwolfSSL fixes that.\\n\\nReported-by: Joshua Rogers\\nCloses #19252\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"TLS: IP address verification, extend test\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/692c7f133e6f9a5053a87b1fffbf3c41697a7742\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"739c09c\",\"date\":\"2025-06-15\",\"message\":\"tests: bundle http clients, de-dupe, enable for MSVC\\n\\nTo make building the http client tests faster, with no duplication, by\\nusing the build method that other test binaries already use.\\n\\nThe difference compared to other tests is that these don't use internal\\nlibcurl headers or code. With the exception of `curl_config.h`, for\\na feature macro.\\n\\nBefore this patch, these tests were built like examples.\\n\\nAlso:\\n- de-duplicate code and give unique names to colliding symbols.\\n- add local getopt implementation and enable all code for MSVC.\\n  Adapted for curl via Public Domain source:\\n  https://github.c... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"tests: bundle http clients, de-dupe, enable for MSVC\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/739c09c8a4111b3ee00b0004f5a3f67e00ba3aeb\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"6d0ee7b\",\"date\":\"2025-12-20\",\"message\":\"curlx: add `curlx_rename()`, fix to support long filenames on Windows\\n\\nMove existing `Curl_rename()` `rename()` wrapper from lib to\\ncurlx/fopen, and make it a curlx macro/function. To allow using\\nthe local worker function to fixup long filenames on Windows.\\n\\nThen fix the Windows-specific rename implementation to support long\\nfilenames. This operation may happen when using a cookie jar, HSTS cache\\nor alt-svc cache, via libcurl or the curl tool.\\n\\nBefore this patch, when passing a long filename to the above options,\\na `<random>.tmp` file was left on the disk without renaming it to the\\nfilename pa... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"curlx: add `curlx_rename()`, fix to support long filenames on Windows\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/6d0ee7b17b0c37c034a78314eda4148104187689\"},{\"author\":\"Dan Fandrich <dan@coneharvesters.com>\",\"commit\":\"36c2c76\",\"date\":\"2026-02-17\",\"message\":\"libcurl-security.md: Fix typos and add a point about URLs\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"libcurl-security.md: Fix typos and add a point about URLs\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/36c2c7626fbe955697cd41cd7e78d88b83575bc1\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"0e8683e\",\"date\":\"2026-01-19\",\"message\":\"cmake: fix variable name for Apple Security framework\\n\\nFollow-up to eefd03c572996e5de4dec4fe295ad6f103e0eefc #18703\\n\\nCloses #20349\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"cmake: fix variable name for Apple Security framework\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/0e8683ee2adfc45ae3f5f4c0690aa1ee66972242\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"25eb34d\",\"date\":\"2025-10-17\",\"message\":\"KNOWN_BUGS: SOCKS-SSPI discards the security context\\n\\nAlso make the verbose log say it\\n\\nPointed out by ZeroPath\\n\\nCloses #19103\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"KNOWN_BUGS: SOCKS-SSPI discards the security context\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/25eb34dd3e7fedb400d3e5b99920936db6711b1e\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"56c892a\",\"date\":\"2025-10-09\",\"message\":\"examples/sessioninfo: do not disable security\\n\\nAlso make it return the curl result code.\\n\\nFollow-up to df70a68984308952dcacf33d11593cb22ad80464 #18909\\nCloses #18969\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"examples/sessioninfo: do not disable security\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/56c892af1f46c7b1c803304ee62e524340007ab2\"},{\"author\":\"Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>\",\"commit\":\"88fc6c4\",\"date\":\"2025-08-20\",\"message\":\"threaded-resolver: fix shutdown\\n\\nChanged strategy to start up and terminate resolver thread.\\n\\nWhen starting up:\\n\\nStart the thread with mutex acquired, wait for signal from thread that\\nit started and has incremented the ref counter. Thread set\\npthread_cancel() to disabled before that and only enables cancelling\\nduring resolving itself. This assure that the ref counter is correct and\\nthe unlinking of the resolve context always happens.\\n\\nWhen shutting down resolving:\\n\\nIf ref counting shows thread has finished, join it, free everything. If\\nthread has not finished, try pthread_cancel() (non Windows... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"threaded-resolver: fix shutdown\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/88fc6c491f043ed184ea2cf1a17b651427fbbbf5\"},{\"author\":\"Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>\",\"commit\":\"8d9aa6d\",\"date\":\"2025-12-02\",\"message\":\"tests/servers: put unix-domain-path inside LOGDIR\\n\\nChange Unix domain socket paths from `/tmp/curl-socksd-<random>` to\\n`/drive/path/to/LOGDIR/PIDDIR/*-uds` to avoid having to create and\\ndelete them before use. Also to use a path which remains an absolute one\\nwhile passed from MSYS2 Perl to native Windows curl tool and test server\\nvia the command-line, and keep pointing to the same location, fixing:\\n```\\n=== Start of file commands.log\\n../src/curl.exe -q --output log/3/curl1468.out --include --trace-ascii log/3/trace1468\\n  --trace-time http://this.is.a.host.name:64405/1468\\n  --proxy socks5h://loc... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"tests/servers: put unix-domain-path inside LOGDIR\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/8d9aa6d6e12cb35b074dd66da454795c8808a41b\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"e496bcf\",\"date\":\"2025-11-12\",\"message\":\"FILEFORMAT.md: drop some text from the `command` section\\n\\nSpecial meanings of URLs became outdated, and it's also no longer\\nnecessary to pass the test number via the URL or domain anymore.\\nDelete the text.\\n\\nFollow-up to c6f1b0ff49268817101ac1734f43074fcc8775b2 #19429\\n\\nCloses #19503\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"FILEFORMAT.md: drop some text from the `command` section\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/e496bcfd0ab63e29e23650d7733adf6e1356f173\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"f0d277c\",\"date\":\"2025-12-11\",\"message\":\"tests: verify new header undfolder\\n\\ntest 798 - incoming cookie header in a folded line\\n\\ntest 1665 - verify HTTP headers without final CRLF. Make sure all complete\\nheaders are delivered even if the reponse is partial\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"tests: verify new header undfolder\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/f0d277cb0e3712ae4edf8f51822ffa99e9b3ec54\"},{\"author\":\"Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>\",\"commit\":\"d1d5855\",\"date\":\"2025-10-09\",\"message\":\"openssl: add comments regarding OCSP verification\\n\\nTo allow future reviewers of \\\"security\\\" reports to more easily find out\\nwhy code is this way.\\n\\nCloses #18962\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"openssl: add comments regarding OCSP verification\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/d1d5855689a29ee0ebc3ee5abd34959cfc8530d4\"},{\"author\":\"Terence Eden <edent@users.noreply.github.com>\",\"commit\":\"a042c67\",\"date\":\"2025-02-08\",\"message\":\"docs: use valid example domain names\\n\\nReplace .site domains and domain.com with valid example domains.\\n\\nFixes #16269\\nCloses #16270\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"docs: use valid example domain names\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/a042c67df3845b55817ee03635e558768868aad4\"},{\"author\":\"Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\",\"commit\":\"c220674\",\"date\":\"2026-02-11\",\"message\":\"windows: tidy up includes, macros, document dependencies\\n\\n- define `SECURITY_WIN32` globally in `curl_setup.h`.\\n  To make sure it applies to all includes.\\n\\n- document which Windows headers require `SECURITY_WIN32`.\\n\\n- stop suppressing MSVC warning:\\n  `C4201 is: nonstandard extension used : nameless struct/union`\\n  The warning is no longer seen in supported build envs with the current\\n  codebase.\\n  Follow-up to 8beff4355956e3d18ceb3afc21c1f3edec82543c #8419\\n\\n- document why `SCHANNEL_USE_BLACKLISTS` is needed.\\n\\n- just define `SCHANNEL_USE_BLACKLISTS`, drop the unnecessary value `1`.\\n\\n- stop defi... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"windows: tidy up includes, macros, document dependencies\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/c220674ac451231e05df1686bd3da473e5c37252\"},{\"author\":\"Daniel Stenberg <daniel@haxx.se>\",\"commit\":\"222fce0\",\"date\":\"2025-12-16\",\"message\":\"cookie. return proper error on OOM\\n\\nFollow-up to a78a07d3a9dc808a51\\n\\nCloses #19992\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"cookie. return proper error on OOM\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/222fce0a50cf296c8633ec0330b074b133e163e1\"},{\"author\":\"James Fuller <jim@webcomposite.com>\",\"commit\":\"af18d8e\",\"date\":\"2026-01-08\",\"message\":\"docs: explicitly call out Slowloris as not a security flaw\\n\\nCloses #20219\",\"repository\":\"github.com/curl/curl\",\"title\":\"docs: explicitly call out Slowloris as not a security flaw\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl/-/commit/af18d8ea1b3c84933aeb508f1c45780bd35437fe\"}],\"limitHit\":true,\"query\":\"type:commit (message:cookie OR message:domain OR message:security) repo:github.com/curl/curl after:2024-12-01 count:20\",\"totalCount\":36}"

"{\"content\":\"427:       }\\n428:     }\\n429:   }\\n430: }\\n431: \\n432: #ifndef USE_LIBPSL\\n433: /* Make sure domain contains a dot or is localhost. */\\n434: static bool bad_domain(const char *domain, size_t len)\\n435: {\\n436:   if((len == 9) && strncasecompare(domain, \\\"localhost\\\", 9))\\n437:     return FALSE;\\n438:   else {\\n439:     /* there must be a dot present, but that dot must not be a trailing dot */\\n440:     char *dot = memchr(domain, '.', len);\\n441:     if(dot) {\\n442:       size_t i = dot - domain;\\n443:       if((len - i) > 1)\\n444:         /* the dot is not the last byte */\\n445:         return FALSE;\\n446:     }\\n447:   }\\n448:   return TRUE;\\n449: }\\n450: #endif\\n451: \\n452: /*\\n453:   RFC 6265 section 4.1.1 says a server should accept this range:\\n454: \\n455:   cookie-octet    = %x21 / %x23-2B / %x2D-3A / %x3C-5B / %x5D-7E\\n456: \\n457:   But Firefox and Chrome as of June 2022 accept space, comma and double-quotes\\n458:   fine. The prime reason for filtering out control bytes is that some HTTP\\n459:   servers return 400 for requests that contain such.\\n460: */\\n461: static bool invalid_octets(const char *p)\\n462: {\\n463:   /* Reject all bytes \\\\x01 - \\\\x1f (*except* \\\\x09, TAB) + \\\\x7f */\\n464:   static const char badoctets[] = {\\n465:     \\\"\\\\x01\\\\x02\\\\x03\\\\x04\\\\x05\\\\x06\\\\x07\\\\x08\\\\x0a\\\"\\n466:     \\\"\\\\x0b\\\\x0c\\\\x0d\\\\x0e\\\\x0f\\\\x10\\\\x11\\\\x12\\\\x13\\\\x14\\\"\\n467:     \\\"\\\\x15\\\\x16\\\\x17\\\\x18\\\\x19\\\\x1a\\\\x1b\\\\x1c\\\\x1d\\\\x1e\\\\x1f\\\\x7f\\\"\\n468:   };\\n469:   size_t len;\\n470:   /* scan for all the octets that are *not* in cookie-octet */\\n471:   len = strcspn(p, badoctets);\\n472:   return p[len] != '\\\\0';\\n473: }\\n474: \\n475: #define CERR_OK            0\\n476: #define CERR_TOO_LONG      1 /* input line too long */\\n477: #define CERR_TAB           2 /* in a wrong place */\\n478: #define CERR_TOO_BIG       3 /* name/value too large */\\n479: #define CERR_BAD           4 /* deemed incorrect */\\n480: #define CERR_NO_SEP        5 /* semicolon problem */\\n481: #define CERR_NO_NAME_VALUE 6 /* name or value problem */\\n482: #define CERR_INVALID_OCTET 7 /* bad content */\\n483: #define CERR_BAD_SECURE    8 /* secure in a bad place */\\n484: #define CERR_OUT_OF_MEMORY 9\\n485: #define CERR_NO_TAILMATCH  10\\n486: #define CERR_COMMENT       11 /* a commented line */\\n487: #define CERR_RANGE         12 /* expire range problem */\\n488: #define CERR_FIELDS        13 /* incomplete netscape line */\\n489: #define CERR_PSL           14 /* a public suffix */\\n490: #define CERR_LIVE_WINS     15\\n491: \\n492: /* The maximum length we accept a date string for the 'expire' keyword. The\\n493:    standard date formats are within the 30 bytes range. This adds an extra\\n494:    margin just to make sure it realistically works with what is used out\\n495:    there.\\n496: */\\n497: #define MAX_DATE_LENGTH 80\\n498: \\n499: static int\\n500: parse_cookie_header(struct Curl_easy *data,\\n501:                     struct Cookie *co,\\n502:                     struct CookieInfo *ci,\\n503:                     const char *ptr,\\n504:                     const char *domain, /* default domain */\\n505:                     const char *path,   /* full path used when this cookie is\\n506:                                            set, used to get default path for\\n507:                                            the cookie unless set */\\n508:                     bool secure)  /* TRUE if connection is over secure\\n509:                                      origin */\\n510: {\\n511:   /* This line was read off an HTTP-header */\\n512:   time_t now;\\n513:   size_t linelength = strlen(ptr);\\n514:   if(linelength > MAX_COOKIE_LINE)\\n515:     /* discard overly long lines at once */\\n516:     return CERR_TOO_LONG;\\n517: \\n518:   now = time(NULL);\\n519:   do {\\n520:     size_t vlen;\\n521:     size_t nlen;\\n522: \\n523:     while(*ptr && ISBLANK(*ptr))\\n524:       ptr++;\\n525: \\n526:     /* we have a <name>=<value> pair or a stand-alone word here */\\n527:     nlen = strcspn(ptr, \\\";\\\\t\\\\r\\\\n=\\\");\\n528:     if(nlen) {\\n529:       bool done = FALSE;\\n530:       bool sep = FALSE;\\n531:       const char *namep = ptr;\\n532:       const char *valuep;\\n533: \\n534:       ptr += nlen;\\n535: \\n536:       /* trim trailing spaces and tabs after name */\\n537:       while(nlen && ISBLANK(namep[nlen - 1]))\\n538:         nlen--;\\n539: \\n540:       if(*ptr == '=') {\\n541:         vlen = strcspn(++ptr, \\\";\\\\r\\\\n\\\");\\n542:         valuep = ptr;\\n543:         sep = TRUE;\\n544:         ptr = &valuep[vlen];\\n545: \\n546:         /* Strip off trailing whitespace from the value */\\n547:         while(vlen && ISBLANK(valuep[vlen-1]))\\n548:           vlen--;\\n549: \\n550:         /* Skip leading whitespace from the value */\\n551: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/curl/curl@34cf9d54a46598c44938aa7598820484d7af7133/-/blob/lib/cookie.c%3FL427-550\"}"
62. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show c9afcecee -- lib/transfer.c 2>/dev/null | head -60",
  "description": "Show the transfer.c fix commit"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "commit c9afcecee94284e754d82354c79e4e37f09bff61\nAuthor: Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\nDate:   Tue Feb 4 18:15:22 2025 +0100\n\n    transfer: fix returning init failures from `xfer_recv_shutdown_started()`\n    \n    Before this patch it returned `CURLE_FAILED_INIT` on init failures, with\n    the value of 2. Fix it to return `false`.\n    \n    Seen with clang 18.1.8:\n    ```\n    ../lib/transfer.c(181,12): warning: integer constant not in range of enumerated type 'bool' [-Wassign-enum]\n      181 |     return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\n          |            ^\n    ../lib/transfer.c(181,12): warning: implicit conversion from enumeration type 'CURLcode' to different enumeration type 'bool' [-Wenum-conversion]\n      181 |     return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\n          |     ~~~~~~ ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n    ../lib/transfer.c(183,12): warning: integer constant not in range of enumerated type 'bool' [-Wassign-enum]\n      183 |     return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\n          |            ^\n    ../lib/transfer.c(183,12): warning: implicit conversion from enumeration type 'CURLcode' to different enumeration type 'bool' [-Wenum-conversion]\n      183 |     return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\n          |     ~~~~~~ ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n    ```\n    \n    Follow-up to 35bf766280a77cde3055e0f4e249ab02a0dcd275 #14253\n    \n    Closes #16170\n\ndiff --git a/lib/transfer.c b/lib/transfer.c\nindex 83e894750..ba4616138 100644\n--- a/lib/transfer.c\n+++ b/lib/transfer.c\n@@ -176,9 +176,9 @@ static bool xfer_recv_shutdown_started(struct Curl_easy *data)\n   int sockindex;\n \n   if(!data || !data->conn)\n-    return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\n+    return false;\n   if(data->conn->sockfd == CURL_SOCKET_BAD)\n-    return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\n+    return false;\n   sockindex = (data->conn->sockfd == data->conn->sock[SECONDARYSOCKET]);\n   return Curl_shutdown_started(data, sockindex);\n }"
}
63. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show 39e21794a -- lib/cookie.c 2>/dev/null | head -80",
  "description": "Show cookie.c crash fix commit"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "commit 39e21794a78dc111b3bee7ad916a4dc34c40d32f\nAuthor: Jay Satiro <raysatiro@yahoo.com>\nDate:   Tue Dec 24 02:18:37 2024 -0500\n\n    cookie: fix crash in netscape cookie parsing\n    \n    - Parse the input string without modifying it.\n    \n    Prior to this change a segfault could occur if the input string was\n    const because the tokenizer modified the input string. For example if\n    the user set CURLOPT_COOKIELIST to a const string then libcurl would\n    likely cause a crash when modifying that string. Even if the string was\n    not const or a crash did not occur there was still the incorrect and\n    unexpected modification of the user's input string.\n    \n    This issue was caused by 30da1f59 (precedes 8.11.0) which refactored\n    some options parsing and eliminated the copy of the input string. Also,\n    an earlier commit f88cc654 incorrectly cast the input pointer when\n    passing it to strtok.\n    \n    Co-authored-by: Daniel Stenberg\n    \n    Closes https://github.com/curl/curl/pull/15826\n\ndiff --git a/lib/cookie.c b/lib/cookie.c\nindex aaa65368c..e1f1c306a 100644\n--- a/lib/cookie.c\n+++ b/lib/cookie.c\n@@ -815,10 +815,9 @@ parse_netscape(struct Cookie *co,\n    * This line is NOT an HTTP header style line, we do offer support for\n    * reading the odd netscape cookies-file format here\n    */\n-  char *ptr;\n-  char *firstptr;\n-  char *tok_buf = NULL;\n+  const char *ptr, *next;\n   int fields;\n+  size_t len;\n \n   /*\n    * In 2008, Internet Explorer introduced HTTP-only cookies to prevent XSS\n@@ -835,29 +834,22 @@ parse_netscape(struct Cookie *co,\n     /* do not even try the comments */\n     return CERR_COMMENT;\n \n-  /* strip off the possible end-of-line characters */\n-  ptr = strchr(lineptr, '\\r');\n-  if(ptr)\n-    *ptr = 0; /* clear it */\n-  ptr = strchr(lineptr, '\\n');\n-  if(ptr)\n-    *ptr = 0; /* clear it */\n-\n-  /* tokenize on TAB */\n-  firstptr = Curl_strtok_r((char *)lineptr, \"\\t\", &tok_buf);\n-\n   /*\n    * Now loop through the fields and init the struct we already have\n    * allocated\n    */\n   fields = 0;\n-  for(ptr = firstptr; ptr;\n-      ptr = Curl_strtok_r(NULL, \"\\t\", &tok_buf), fields++) {\n+  for(next = lineptr; next; fields++) {\n+    ptr = next;\n+    len = strcspn(ptr, \"\\t\\r\\n\");\n+    next = (ptr[len] == '\\t' ? &ptr[len + 1] : NULL);\n     switch(fields) {\n     case 0:\n-      if(ptr[0]=='.') /* skip preceding dots */\n+      if(ptr[0]=='.') { /* skip preceding dots */\n         ptr++;\n-      co->domain = strdup(ptr);\n+        len--;\n+      }\n+      co->domain = Curl_memdup0(ptr, len);\n       if(!co->domain)\n         return CERR_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n       break;\n@@ -867,13 +859,13 @@ parse_netscape(struct Cookie *co,"
}

  "command": "git show 86ee64901 -- lib/transfer.c 2>/dev/null | head -60",
  "description": "Show transfer.c false->FALSE commit"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "commit 86ee64901f9a8e78d56b50f83e27d20691809764\nAuthor: Viktor Szakats <commit@vsz.me>\nDate:   Wed Feb 5 02:26:01 2025 +0100\n\n    transfer: replace `false` with `FALSE`\n    \n    Follow-up to c9afcecee94284e754d82354c79e4e37f09bff61 #16170\n    \n    Closes #16177\n\ndiff --git a/lib/transfer.c b/lib/transfer.c\nindex ba4616138..c4b23a8f0 100644\n--- a/lib/transfer.c\n+++ b/lib/transfer.c\n@@ -176,9 +176,9 @@ static bool xfer_recv_shutdown_started(struct Curl_easy *data)\n   int sockindex;\n \n   if(!data || !data->conn)\n-    return false;\n+    return FALSE;\n   if(data->conn->sockfd == CURL_SOCKET_BAD)\n-    return false;\n+    return FALSE;\n   sockindex = (data->conn->sockfd == data->conn->sock[SECONDARYSOCKET]);\n   return Curl_shutdown_started(data, sockindex);\n }"
}

66. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/lib/cookie.c",
  "limit": 100,
  "offset": 125
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "  free(co->name);\n  free(co->value);\n  free(co);\n}\n\nstatic bool cookie_tailmatch(const char *cookie_domain,\n                             size_t cookie_domain_len,\n                             const char *hostname)\n{\n  size_t hostname_len = strlen(hostname);\n\n  if(hostname_len < cookie_domain_len)\n    return FALSE;\n\n  if(!strncasecompare(cookie_domain,\n                      hostname + hostname_len-cookie_domain_len,\n                      cookie_domain_len))\n    return FALSE;\n\n  /*\n   * A lead char of cookie_domain is not '.'.\n   * RFC6265 4.1.2.3. The Domain Attribute says:\n   * For example, if the value of the Domain attribute is\n   * \"example.com\", the user agent will include the cookie in the Cookie\n   * header when making HTTP requests to example.com, www.example.com, and\n   * www.corp.example.com.\n   */\n  if(hostname_len == cookie_domain_len)\n    return TRUE;\n  if('.' == *(hostname + hostname_len - cookie_domain_len - 1))\n    return TRUE;\n  return FALSE;\n}\n\n/*\n * matching cookie path and URL path\n * RFC6265 5.1.4 Paths and Path-Match\n */\nstatic bool pathmatch(const char *cookie_path, const char *request_uri)\n{\n  size_t cookie_path_len;\n  size_t uri_path_len;\n  char *uri_path = NULL;\n  char *pos;\n  bool ret = FALSE;\n\n  /* cookie_path must not have last '/' separator. ex: /sample */\n  cookie_path_len = strlen(cookie_path);\n  if(1 == cookie_path_len) {\n    /* cookie_path must be '/' */\n    return TRUE;\n  }\n\n  uri_path = strdup(request_uri);\n  if(!uri_path)\n    return FALSE;\n  pos = strchr(uri_path, '?');\n  if(pos)\n    *pos = 0x0;\n\n  /* #-fragments are already cut off! */\n  if(0 == strlen(uri_path) || uri_path[0] != '/') {\n    strstore(&uri_path, \"/\", 1);\n    if(!uri_path)\n      return FALSE;\n  }\n\n  /*\n   * here, RFC6265 5.1.4 says\n   *  4. Output the characters of the uri-path from the first character up\n   *     to, but not including, the right-most %x2F (\"/\").\n   *  but URL path /hoge?fuga=xxx means /hoge/index.cgi?fuga=xxx in some site\n   *  without redirect.\n   *  Ignore this algorithm because /hoge is uri path for this case\n   *  (uri path is not /).\n   */\n\n  uri_path_len = strlen(uri_path);\n\n  if(uri_path_len < cookie_path_len) {\n    ret = FALSE;\n    goto pathmatched;\n  }\n\n  /* not using checkprefix() because matching should be case-sensitive */\n  if(strncmp(cookie_path, uri_path, cookie_path_len)) {\n    ret = FALSE;\n    goto pathmatched;\n  }\n\n  /* The cookie-path and the uri-path are identical. */\n  if(cookie_path_len == uri_path_len) {\n    ret = TRUE;\n    goto pathmatched;\n  }\n\n  /* here, cookie_path_len < uri_path_len */\n  if(uri_path[cookie_path_len] == '/') {\n    ret = TRUE;\n    goto pathmatched;",
    "filePath": "/workspace/lib/cookie.c",
    "numLines": 100,
    "startLine": 125,
    "totalLines": 1751
  },
  "type": "text"
}
67. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/lib/cookie.c",
  "limit": 100,
  "offset": 825
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "  data->req.setcookies++;\n  return CERR_OK;\n}\n\nstatic int\nparse_netscape(struct Cookie *co,\n               struct CookieInfo *ci,\n               const char *lineptr,\n               bool secure)  /* TRUE if connection is over secure\n                                origin */\n{\n  /*\n   * This line is NOT an HTTP header style line, we do offer support for\n   * reading the odd netscape cookies-file format here\n   */\n  const char *ptr, *next;\n  int fields;\n  size_t len;\n\n  /*\n   * In 2008, Internet Explorer introduced HTTP-only cookies to prevent XSS\n   * attacks. Cookies marked httpOnly are not accessible to JavaScript. In\n   * Firefox's cookie files, they are prefixed #HttpOnly_ and the rest\n   * remains as usual, so we skip 10 characters of the line.\n   */\n  if(strncmp(lineptr, \"#HttpOnly_\", 10) == 0) {\n    lineptr += 10;\n    co->httponly = TRUE;\n  }\n\n  if(lineptr[0]=='#')\n    /* do not even try the comments */\n    return CERR_COMMENT;\n\n  /*\n   * Now loop through the fields and init the struct we already have\n   * allocated\n   */\n  fields = 0;\n  for(next = lineptr; next; fields++) {\n    ptr = next;\n    len = strcspn(ptr, \"\\t\\r\\n\");\n    next = (ptr[len] == '\\t' ? &ptr[len + 1] : NULL);\n    switch(fields) {\n    case 0:\n      if(ptr[0]=='.') { /* skip preceding dots */\n        ptr++;\n        len--;\n      }\n      co->domain = Curl_memdup0(ptr, len);\n      if(!co->domain)\n        return CERR_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n      break;\n    case 1:\n      /*\n       * flag: A TRUE/FALSE value indicating if all machines within a given\n       * domain can access the variable. Set TRUE when the cookie says\n       * .domain.com and to false when the domain is complete www.domain.com\n       */\n      co->tailmatch = !!strncasecompare(ptr, \"TRUE\", len);\n      break;\n    case 2:\n      /* The file format allows the path field to remain not filled in */\n      if(strncmp(\"TRUE\", ptr, len) && strncmp(\"FALSE\", ptr, len)) {\n        /* only if the path does not look like a boolean option! */\n        co->path = Curl_memdup0(ptr, len);\n        if(!co->path)\n          return CERR_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n        else {\n          co->spath = sanitize_cookie_path(co->path);\n          if(!co->spath)\n            return CERR_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n        }\n        break;\n      }\n      /* this does not look like a path, make one up! */\n      co->path = strdup(\"/\");\n      if(!co->path)\n        return CERR_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n      co->spath = strdup(\"/\");\n      if(!co->spath)\n        return CERR_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n      fields++; /* add a field and fall down to secure */\n      FALLTHROUGH();\n    case 3:\n      co->secure = FALSE;\n      if(strncasecompare(ptr, \"TRUE\", len)) {\n        if(secure || ci->running)\n          co->secure = TRUE;\n        else\n          return CERR_BAD_SECURE;\n      }\n      break;\n    case 4:\n      {\n        char *endp;\n        const char *p;\n        /* make sure curlx_strtoofft won't read past the current field */\n        for(p = ptr; p < &ptr[len] && ISDIGIT(*p); ++p)\n          ;",
    "filePath": "/workspace/lib/cookie.c",
    "numLines": 100,
    "startLine": 825,
    "totalLines": 1751
  },
  "type": "text"
}

  "stdout": "39e21794a cookie: fix crash in netscape cookie parsing\nc9afcecee transfer: fix returning init failures from `xfer_recv_shutdown_started()`\n86ee64901 transfer: replace `false` with `FALSE`"
}

{

}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "35bf76628 http2: improved upload eos handling\ncommit 35bf766280a77cde3055e0f4e249ab02a0dcd275\nAuthor: Stefan Eissing <stefan@eissing.org>\nDate:   Sun Aug 4 09:51:26 2024 +0200\n\n    http2: improved upload eos handling\n    \n    - replace the counting of upload lengths with the new eos send flag\n    - improve frequency of stream draining to happen less on events where it\n      is not needed\n    - this PR is based on #14220\n    \n    http2, cf-h2-proxy: fix EAGAINed out buffer\n    - in adjust pollset and shutdown handling, a non-empty `ctx->outbufq`\n      must trigger send polling, irregardless of http/2 flow control\n    - in http2, fix retry handling of blocked GOAWAY frame\n    \n    test case improvement:\n    - let client 'upload-pausing' handle http versions\n    \n    Closes #14253"
}

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "/*\n * Check to see if CURLOPT_TIMECONDITION was met by comparing the time of the\n * remote document with the time provided by CURLOPT_TIMEVAL\n */\nbool Curl_meets_timecondition(struct Curl_easy *data, time_t timeofdoc)\n{\n  if((timeofdoc == 0) || (data->set.timevalue == 0))\n    return TRUE;\n\n  switch(data->set.timecondition) {\n  case CURL_TIMECOND_IFMODSINCE:\n  default:\n    if(timeofdoc <= data->set.timevalue) {\n      infof(data,\n            \"The requested document is not new enough\");\n      data->info.timecond = TRUE;\n      return FALSE;\n    }\n    break;\n  case CURL_TIMECOND_IFUNMODSINCE:\n    if(timeofdoc >= data->set.timevalue) {\n      infof(data,\n            \"The requested document is not old enough\");\n      data->info.timecond = TRUE;\n      return FALSE;\n    }\n    break;\n  }\n\n  return TRUE;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode xfer_recv_shutdown(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done)\n{\n  int sockindex;\n\n  if(!data || !data->conn)\n    return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\n  if(data->conn->sockfd == CURL_SOCKET_BAD)\n    return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\n  sockindex = (data->conn->sockfd == data->conn->sock[SECONDARYSOCKET]);\n  return Curl_conn_shutdown(data, sockindex, done);\n}\n\nstatic bool xfer_recv_shutdown_started(struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  int sockindex;\n\n  if(!data || !data->conn)\n    return FALSE;\n  if(data->conn->sockfd == CURL_SOCKET_BAD)\n    return FALSE;\n  sockindex = (data->conn->sockfd == data->conn->sock[SECONDARYSOCKET]);\n  return Curl_shutdown_started(data, sockindex);\n}\n\nCURLcode Curl_xfer_send_shutdown(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done)\n{\n  int sockindex;\n\n  if(!data || !data->conn)\n    return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\n  if(data->conn->writesockfd == CURL_SOCKET_BAD)\n    return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\n  sockindex = (data->conn->writesockfd == data->conn->sock[SECONDARYSOCKET]);\n  return Curl_conn_shutdown(data, sockindex, done);\n}\n\n/**\n * Receive raw response data for the transfer.\n * @param data         the transfer\n * @param buf          buffer to keep response data received\n * @param blen         length of `buf`\n * @param eos_reliable if EOS detection in underlying connection is reliable\n * @param err error    code in case of -1 return\n * @return number of bytes read or -1 for error\n */\nstatic ssize_t xfer_recv_resp(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                              char *buf, size_t blen,\n                              bool eos_reliable,",
    "filePath": "/workspace/lib/transfer.c",
    "numLines": 80,
    "startLine": 130,
    "totalLines": 982
  },
  "type": "text"
}

{
  "file": {
    "content": "static int passwd_callback(char *buf, int num, int encrypting,\n                           void *global_passwd)\n{\n  DEBUGASSERT(0 == encrypting);\n\n  if(!encrypting && num >= 0) {\n    int klen = curlx_uztosi(strlen((char *)global_passwd));\n    if(num > klen) {\n      memcpy(buf, global_passwd, klen + 1);\n      return klen;\n    }\n  }\n  return 0;\n}\n\n/*\n * rand_enough() returns TRUE if we have seeded the random engine properly.\n */\nstatic bool rand_enough(void)\n{\n  return 0 != RAND_status();\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode ossl_seed(struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  /* This might get called before it has been added to a multi handle */\n  if(data->multi && data->multi->ssl_seeded)\n    return CURLE_OK;\n\n  if(rand_enough()) {\n    /* OpenSSL 1.1.0+ should return here */\n    if(data->multi)\n      data->multi->ssl_seeded = TRUE;\n    return CURLE_OK;\n  }\n#ifdef HAVE_RANDOM_INIT_BY_DEFAULT\n  /* with OpenSSL 1.1.0+, a failed RAND_status is a showstopper */\n  failf(data, \"Insufficient randomness\");\n  return CURLE_SSL_CONNECT_ERROR;\n#else",
    "filePath": "/workspace/lib/vtls/openssl.c",
    "numLines": 40,
    "startLine": 987,
    "totalLines": 5536
  },

}
74. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/lib/vtls/openssl.c",
  "limit": 180,
  "offset": 2234
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": " * Match subjectAltName against the hostname.\n */\nstatic bool subj_alt_hostcheck(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                               const char *match_pattern,\n                               size_t matchlen,\n                               const char *hostname,\n                               size_t hostlen,\n                               const char *dispname)\n{\n#ifdef CURL_DISABLE_VERBOSE_STRINGS\n  (void)dispname;\n  (void)data;\n#endif\n  if(Curl_cert_hostcheck(match_pattern, matchlen, hostname, hostlen)) {\n    infof(data, \" subjectAltName: host \\\"%s\\\" matched cert's \\\"%s\\\"\",\n          dispname, match_pattern);\n    return TRUE;\n  }\n  return FALSE;\n}\n\n/* Quote from RFC2818 section 3.1 \"Server Identity\"\n\n   If a subjectAltName extension of type dNSName is present, that MUST\n   be used as the identity. Otherwise, the (most specific) Common Name\n   field in the Subject field of the certificate MUST be used. Although\n   the use of the Common Name is existing practice, it is deprecated and\n   Certification Authorities are encouraged to use the dNSName instead.\n\n   Matching is performed using the matching rules specified by\n   [RFC2459]. If more than one identity of a given type is present in\n   the certificate (e.g., more than one dNSName name, a match in any one\n   of the set is considered acceptable.) Names may contain the wildcard\n   character * which is considered to match any single domain name\n   component or component fragment. E.g., *.a.com matches foo.a.com but\n   not bar.foo.a.com. f*.com matches foo.com but not bar.com.\n\n   In some cases, the URI is specified as an IP address rather than a\n   hostname. In this case, the iPAddress subjectAltName must be present\n   in the certificate and must exactly match the IP in the URI.\n\n   This function is now used from ngtcp2 (QUIC) as well.\n*/\nstatic CURLcode ossl_verifyhost(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                struct connectdata *conn,\n                                struct ssl_peer *peer, X509 *server_cert)\n{\n  bool matched = FALSE;\n  int target; /* target type, GEN_DNS or GEN_IPADD */\n  size_t addrlen = 0;\n  STACK_OF(GENERAL_NAME) *altnames;\n#ifdef USE_IPV6\n  struct in6_addr addr;\n#else\n  struct in_addr addr;\n#endif\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n  bool dNSName = FALSE; /* if a dNSName field exists in the cert */\n  bool iPAddress = FALSE; /* if an iPAddress field exists in the cert */\n  size_t hostlen;\n\n  (void)conn;\n  hostlen = strlen(peer->hostname);\n  switch(peer->type) {\n  case CURL_SSL_PEER_IPV4:\n    if(!Curl_inet_pton(AF_INET, peer->hostname, &addr))\n      return CURLE_PEER_FAILED_VERIFICATION;\n    target = GEN_IPADD;\n    addrlen = sizeof(struct in_addr);\n    break;\n#ifdef USE_IPV6\n  case CURL_SSL_PEER_IPV6:\n    if(!Curl_inet_pton(AF_INET6, peer->hostname, &addr))\n      return CURLE_PEER_FAILED_VERIFICATION;\n    target = GEN_IPADD;\n    addrlen = sizeof(struct in6_addr);\n    break;\n#endif\n  case CURL_SSL_PEER_DNS:\n    target = GEN_DNS;\n    break;\n  default:\n    DEBUGASSERT(0);\n    failf(data, \"unexpected ssl peer type: %d\", peer->type);\n    return CURLE_PEER_FAILED_VERIFICATION;\n  }\n\n  /* get a \"list\" of alternative names */\n  altnames = X509_get_ext_d2i(server_cert, NID_subject_alt_name, NULL, NULL);\n\n  if(altnames) {\n#if defined(OPENSSL_IS_BORINGSSL) || defined(OPENSSL_IS_AWSLC)\n    size_t numalts;\n    size_t i;\n#else\n    int numalts;\n    int i;\n#endif\n    bool dnsmatched = FALSE;\n    bool ipmatched = FALSE;\n\n    /* get amount of alternatives, RFC2459 claims there MUST be at least\n       one, but we do not depend on it... */\n    numalts = sk_GENERAL_NAME_num(altnames);\n\n    /* loop through all alternatives - until a dnsmatch */\n    for(i = 0; (i < numalts) && !dnsmatched; i++) {\n      /* get a handle to alternative name number i */\n      const GENERAL_NAME *check = sk_GENERAL_NAME_value(altnames, i);\n\n      if(check->type == GEN_DNS)\n        dNSName = TRUE;\n      else if(check->type == GEN_IPADD)\n        iPAddress = TRUE;\n\n      /* only check alternatives of the same type the target is */\n      if(check->type == target) {\n        /* get data and length */\n        const char *altptr = (char *)ASN1_STRING_get0_data(check->d.ia5);\n        size_t altlen = (size_t) ASN1_STRING_length(check->d.ia5);\n\n        switch(target) {\n        case GEN_DNS: /* name/pattern comparison */\n          /* The OpenSSL manpage explicitly says: \"In general it cannot be\n             assumed that the data returned by ASN1_STRING_data() is null\n             terminated or does not contain embedded nulls.\" But also that\n             \"The actual format of the data will depend on the actual string\n             type itself: for example for an IA5String the data will be ASCII\"\n\n             It has been however verified that in 0.9.6 and 0.9.7, IA5String\n             is always null-terminated.\n          */\n          if((altlen == strlen(altptr)) &&\n             /* if this is not true, there was an embedded zero in the name\n                string and we cannot match it. */\n             subj_alt_hostcheck(data, altptr, altlen,\n                                peer->hostname, hostlen,\n                                peer->dispname)) {\n            dnsmatched = TRUE;\n          }\n          break;\n\n        case GEN_IPADD: /* IP address comparison */\n          /* compare alternative IP address if the data chunk is the same size\n             our server IP address is */\n          if((altlen == addrlen) && !memcmp(altptr, &addr, altlen)) {\n            ipmatched = TRUE;\n            infof(data,\n                  \" subjectAltName: host \\\"%s\\\" matched cert's IP address!\",\n                  peer->dispname);\n          }\n          break;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n    GENERAL_NAMES_free(altnames);\n\n    if(dnsmatched || ipmatched)\n      matched = TRUE;\n  }\n\n  if(matched)\n    /* an alternative name matched */\n    ;\n  else if(dNSName || iPAddress) {\n    const char *tname = (peer->type == CURL_SSL_PEER_DNS) ? \"hostname\" :\n                        (peer->type == CURL_SSL_PEER_IPV4) ?\n                        \"ipv4 address\" : \"ipv6 address\";\n    infof(data, \" subjectAltName does not match %s %s\", tname, peer->dispname);\n    failf(data, \"SSL: no alternative certificate subject name matches \"\n          \"target %s '%s'\", tname, peer->dispname);\n    result = CURLE_PEER_FAILED_VERIFICATION;\n  }\n  else {\n    /* we have to look to the last occurrence of a commonName in the\n       distinguished one to get the most significant one. */\n    int i = -1;\n    unsigned char *cn = NULL;\n    int cnlen = 0;\n    bool free_cn = FALSE;",
    "filePath": "/workspace/lib/vtls/openssl.c",
    "numLines": 180,
    "startLine": 2234,
    "totalLines": 5536
  },
  "type": "text"
}

Read lib/cookie.c from the MASTER branch and show:\n   - The `cookie_tailmatch` function (around lines 130-160)\n\n3. Read lib/transfer.c from MASTER and show:\n   - The `xfer_recv_shutdown` and `xfer_recv_shutdown_started` functions\n\n4. Read lib/base64.c from MASTER and show:\n   - The `Curl_base64_decode` function\n   - The `base64_encode` function (the static helper)\n\nPlease provide the EXACT code with line numbers for each function from the MASTER branch.",
