if the connection has already been established */\n  if(ssl_connection_complete == connssl->state) {\n    *done = TRUE;\n    return CURLE_OK;\n  }\n\n  if(ssl_connect_1 == connssl->connecting_state) {\n    /* Find out how much more time we are allowed */\n    const timediff_t timeout_ms = Curl_timeleft(data, NULL, TRUE);\n\n    if(timeout_ms < 0) {\n      /* no need to continue if time is already up */\n      failf(data, \"SSL connection timeout\");\n      return CURLE_OPERATION_TIMEDOUT;\n    }\n\n    result = ossl_connect_step1(cf, data);\n    if(result)\n      goto out;\n  }\n\n  while(ssl_connect_2 == connssl->connecting_state) {\n\n    /* check allowed time left */\n    const timediff_t timeout_ms = Curl_timeleft(data, NULL, TRUE);\n\n    if(timeout_ms < 0) {\n      /* no need to continue if time already is up */\n      failf(data, \"SSL connection timeout\");\n      result = CURLE_OPERATION_TIMEDOUT;\n      goto out;\n    }\n\n    /* if ssl is expecting something, check if it is available. */\n    if(!nonblocking && connssl->io_need) {\n      curl_socket_t writefd = (connssl->io_need & CURL_SSL_IO_NEED_SEND) ?\n        sockfd : CURL_SOCKET_BAD;\n      curl_socket_t readfd = (connssl->io_need & CURL_SSL_IO_NEED_RECV) ?\n        sockfd : CURL_SOCKET_BAD;\n\n      what = Curl_socket_check(readfd, CURL_SOCKET_BAD, writefd,\n                               timeout_ms);\n      if(what < 0) {\n        /* fatal error */\n        failf(data, \"select/poll on SSL socket, errno: %d\", SOCKERRNO);\n        result = CURLE_SSL_CONNECT_ERROR;\n        goto out;\n      }\n      if(0 == what) {\n        /* timeout */\n        failf(data, \"SSL connection timeout\");\n        result = CURLE_OPERATION_TIMEDOUT;\n        goto out;\n      }\n      /* socket is readable or writable */\n    }\n\n    /* Run transaction, and return to the caller if it failed or if this\n     * connection is done nonblocking and this loop would execute again. This\n     * permits the owner of a multi handle to abort a connection attempt\n     * before step2 has completed while ensuring that a client using select()\n     * or epoll() will always have a valid fdset to wait on.\n     */\n    result = ossl_connect_step2(cf, data);\n    if(result || (nonblocking && (ssl_connect_2 == connssl->connecting_state)))\n      goto out;\n\n  } /* repeat step2 until all transactions are done. */\n\n  if(ssl_connect_3 == connssl->connecting_state) {\n    result = ossl_connect_step3(cf, data);\n    if(result)\n      goto out;\n  }\n\n  if(ssl_connect_done == connssl->connecting_state) {\n    connssl->state = ssl_connection_complete;\n    *done = TRUE;\n  }\n  else\n    *done = FALSE;\n\n  /* Reset our connect state machine */\n  connssl->connecting_state = ssl_connect_1;\n\nout:\n  return result;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode ossl_connect_nonblocking(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                                         struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                         bool *done)\n{\n  return ossl_connect_common(cf, data, TRUE, done);\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode ossl_connect(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                             struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  CURLcode result;\n  bool done = FALSE;\n\n  result = ossl_connect_common(cf, data, FALSE, &done);\n  if(result)\n    return result;\n\n  DEBUGASSERT(done);\n\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n\nstatic bool ossl_data_pending(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                              const struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  struct ssl_connect_data *connssl = cf->ctx;\n  struct ossl_ctx *octx = (struct ossl_ctx *)connssl->backend;\n\n  (void)data;\n  DEBUGASSERT(connssl && octx);\n  if(octx->ssl && SSL_pending(octx->ssl))\n    return TRUE;\n  return FALSE;\n}\n\nstatic ssize_t ossl_send(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                         struct Curl_easy *data,\n                         const void *mem,\n                         size_t len,\n                         CURLcode *curlcode)\n{\n  /* SSL_write() is said to return 'int' while write() and send() returns\n     'size_t' */\n  int err;\n  char error_buffer[256];\n  sslerr_t sslerror;\n  int memlen;\n  int rc;\n  struct ssl_connect_data *connssl = cf->ctx;\n  struct ossl_ctx *octx = (struct ossl_ctx *)connssl->backend;\n\n  (void)data;\n  DEBUGASSERT(octx);\n\n  ERR_clear_error();\n\n  connssl->io_need = CURL_SSL_IO_NEED_NONE;\n  memlen = (len > (size_t)INT_MAX) ? INT_MAX : (int)len;\n  rc = SSL_write(octx->ssl, mem, memlen);\n\n  if(rc <= 0) {\n    err = SSL_get_error(octx->ssl, rc);\n\n    switch(err) {\n    case SSL_ERROR_WANT_READ:\n      connssl->io_need = CURL_SSL_IO_NEED_RECV;\n      *curlcode = CURLE_AGAIN;\n      rc = -1;\n      goto out;\n    case SSL_ERROR_WANT_WRITE:\n      *curlcode = CURLE_AGAIN;\n      rc = -1;\n      goto out;\n    case SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL:\n    {\n      int sockerr = SOCKERRNO;\n\n      if(octx->io_result == CURLE_AGAIN) {\n        *curlcode = CURLE_AGAIN;\n        rc = -1;\n        goto out;\n      }\n      sslerror = ERR_get_error();\n      if(sslerror)\n        ossl_strerror(sslerror, error_buffer, sizeof(error_buffer));\n      else if(sockerr)\n        Curl_strerror(sockerr, error_buffer, sizeof(error_buffer));\n      else\n        msnprintf(error_buffer, sizeof(error_buffer), \"%s\",\n                  SSL_ERROR_to_str(err));\n\n      failf(data, OSSL_PACKAGE \" SSL_write: %s, errno %d\",\n            error_buffer, sockerr);\n      *curlcode = CURLE_SEND_ERROR;\n      rc = -1;\n      goto out;\n    }\n    case SSL_ERROR_SSL: {\n      /*  A failure in the SSL library occurred, usually a protocol error.\n          The OpenSSL error queue contains more information on the error. */\n      sslerror = ERR_get_error();\n      failf(data, \"SSL_write() error: %s\",\n            ossl_strerror(sslerror, error_buffer, sizeof(error_buffer)));\n      *curlcode = CURLE_SEND_ERROR;\n      rc = -1;\n      goto out;\n    }\n    default:\n      /* a true error */\n      failf(data, OSSL_PACKAGE \" SSL_write: %s, errno %d\",\n            SSL_ERROR_to_str(err), SOCKERRNO);\n      *curlcode = CURLE_SEND_ERROR;\n      rc = -1;\n      goto out;\n    }\n  }\n  *curlcode = CURLE_OK;\n\nout:\n  return (ssize_t)rc; /* number of bytes */\n}\n\nstatic ssize_t ossl_recv(struct Curl_cfilter *cf,\n                         struct Curl_easy *data,   /* transfer */\n                         char *buf,                /* store read data here */\n                         size_t buffersize,        /* max amount to read */\n                         CURLcode *curlcode)\n{\n  char error_buffer[256];\n  unsigned long sslerror;\n  ssize_t nread;\n  int buffsize;\n  struct connectdata *conn = cf->conn;\n  struct ssl_connect_data *connssl = cf->ctx;\n  struct ossl_ctx *octx = (struct ossl_ctx *)connssl->backend;\n\n  (void)data;\n  DEBUGASSERT(octx);\n\n  ERR_clear_error();\n\n  connssl->io_need = CURL_SSL_IO_NEED_NONE;\n  buffsize = (buffersize > (size_t)INT_MAX) ? INT_MAX : (int)buffersize;\n  nread = (ssize_t)SSL_read(octx->ssl, buf, buffsize);\n\n  if(nread <= 0) {\n    /* failed SSL_read */\n    int err = SSL_get_error(octx->ssl, (int)nread);\n\n    switch(err) {\n    case SSL_ERROR_NONE: /* this is not an error */\n      break;\n    case SSL_ERROR_ZERO_RETURN: /* no more data */\n      /* close_notify alert */\n      if(cf->sockindex == FIRSTSOCKET)\n        /* mark the connection for close if it is indeed the control\n           connection */\n        connclose(conn, \"TLS close_notify\");\n      break;\n    case SSL_ERROR_WANT_READ:\n      *curlcode = CURLE_AGAIN;\n      nread = -1;\n      goto out;\n    case SSL_ERROR_WANT_WRITE:\n      connssl->io_need = CURL_SSL_IO_NEED_SEND;\n      *curlcode = CURLE_AGAIN;\n      nread = -1;\n      goto out;\n    default:\n      /* openssl/ssl.h for SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL says \"look at error stack/return\n         value/errno\" */\n      /* https://docs.openssl.org/master/man3/ERR_get_error/ */\n      if(octx->io_result == CURLE_AGAIN) {\n        *curlcode = CURLE_AGAIN;\n        nread = -1;\n        goto out;\n      }\n      sslerror = ERR_get_error();\n      if((nread < 0) || sslerror) {\n        /* If the return code was negative or there actually is an error in the\n           queue */\n        int sockerr = SOCKERRNO;\n        if(sslerror)\n          ossl_strerror(sslerror, error_buffer, sizeof(error_buffer));\n        else if(sockerr && err == SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL)\n          Curl_strerror(sockerr, error_buffer, sizeof(error_buffer));\n        else\n          msnprintf(error_buffer, sizeof(error_buffer), \"%s\",\n                    SSL_ERROR_to_str(err));\n        failf(data, OSSL_PACKAGE \" SSL_read: %s, errno %d\",\n              error_buffer, sockerr);\n        *curlcode = CURLE_RECV_ERROR;\n        nread = -1;\n        goto out;\n      }\n      /* For debug builds be a little stricter and error on any\n         SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL. For example a server may have closed the connection\n         abruptly without a close_notify alert. For compatibility with older\n         peers we do not do this by default. #4624\n\n         We can use this to gauge how many users may be affected, and\n         if it goes ok eventually transition to allow in dev and release with\n         the newest OpenSSL: #if (OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x10101000L) */\n#ifdef DEBUGBUILD\n      if(err == SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL) {\n        int sockerr = SOCKERRNO;\n        if(sockerr)\n          Curl_strerror(sockerr, error_buffer, sizeof(error_buffer));\n        else {\n          msnprintf(error_buffer, sizeof(error_buffer),\n                    \"Connection closed abruptly\");\n        }\n        failf(data, OSSL_PACKAGE \" SSL_read: %s, errno %d\"\n              \" (Fatal because this is a curl debug build)\",\n              error_buffer, sockerr);\n        *curlcode = CURLE_RECV_ERROR;\n        nread = -1;\n        goto out;\n      }\n#endif\n    }\n  }\n\nout:\n  return nread;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode ossl_get_channel_binding(struct Curl_easy *data, int sockindex,\n                                         struct dynbuf *binding)\n{\n  /* required for X509_get_signature_nid support */\n#if OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER > 0x10100000L\n  X509 *cert;\n  int algo_nid;\n  const EVP_MD *algo_type;\n  const char *algo_name;\n  unsigned int length;\n  unsigned char buf[EVP_MAX_MD_SIZE];\n\n  const char prefix[] = \"tls-server-end-point:\";\n  struct connectdata *conn = data->conn;\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf = conn->cfilter[sockindex];\n  struct ossl_ctx *octx = NULL;\n\n  do {\n    const struct Curl_cftype *cft = cf->cft;\n    struct ssl_connect_data *connssl = cf->ctx;\n\n    if(cft->name && !strcmp(cft->name, \"SSL\")) {\n      octx = (struct ossl_ctx *)connssl->backend;\n      break;\n    }\n\n    if(cf->next)\n      cf = cf->next;\n\n  } while(cf->next);\n\n  if(!octx) {\n    failf(data, \"Failed to find the SSL filter\");\n    return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n  }\n\n  cert = SSL_get1_peer_certificate(octx->ssl);\n  if(!cert) {\n    /* No server certificate, don't do channel binding */\n    return CURLE_OK;\n  }\n\n  if(!OBJ_find_sigid_algs(X509_get_signature_nid(cert), &algo_nid, NULL)) {\n    failf(data,\n          \"Unable to find digest NID for certificate signature algorithm\");\n    return CURLE_SSL_INVALIDCERTSTATUS;\n  }\n\n  /* https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5929#section-4.1 */\n  if(algo_nid == NID_md5 || algo_nid == NID_sha1) {\n    algo_type = EVP_sha256();\n  }\n  else {\n    algo_type = EVP_get_digestbynid(algo_nid);\n    if(!algo_type) {\n      algo_name = OBJ_nid2sn(algo_nid);\n      failf(data, \"Could not find digest algorithm %s (NID %d)\",\n            algo_name ? algo_name : \"(null)\", algo_nid);\n      return CURLE_SSL_INVALIDCERTSTATUS;\n    }\n  }\n\n  if(!X509_digest(cert, algo_type, buf, &length)) {\n    failf(data, \"X509_digest() failed\");\n    return CURLE_SSL_INVALIDCERTSTATUS;\n  }\n\n  /* Append \"tls-server-end-point:\" */\n  if(Curl_dyn_addn(binding, prefix, sizeof(prefix) - 1) != CURLE_OK)\n    return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n  /* Append digest */\n  if(Curl_dyn_addn(binding, buf, length))\n    return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n\n  return CURLE_OK;\n#else\n  /* No X509_get_signature_nid support */\n  (void)data; /* unused */\n  (void)sockindex; /* unused */\n  (void)binding; /* unused */\n  return CURLE_OK;\n#endif\n}\n\nsize_t Curl_ossl_version(char *buffer, size_t size)\n{\n#ifdef LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER\n#ifdef HAVE_OPENSSL_VERSION\n  char *p;\n  size_t count;\n  const char *ver = OpenSSL_version(OPENSSL_VERSION);\n  const char expected[] = OSSL_PACKAGE \" \"; /* ie \"LibreSSL \" */\n  if(strncasecompare(ver, expected, sizeof(expected) - 1)) {\n    ver += sizeof(expected) - 1;\n  }\n  count = msnprintf(buffer, size, \"%s/%s\", OSSL_PACKAGE, ver);\n  for(p = buffer; *p; ++p) {\n    if(ISBLANK(*p))\n      *p = '_';\n  }\n  return count;\n#else\n  return msnprintf(buffer, size, \"%s/%lx.%lx.%lx\",\n                   OSSL_PACKAGE,\n                   (LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER >> 28) & 0xf,\n                   (LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER >> 20) & 0xff,\n                   (LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER >> 12) & 0xff);\n#endif\n#elif defined(OPENSSL_IS_BORINGSSL)\n#ifdef CURL_BORINGSSL_VERSION\n  return msnprintf(buffer, size, \"%s/%s\",\n                   OSSL_PACKAGE,\n                   CURL_BORINGSSL_VERSION);\n#else\n  return msnprintf(buffer, size, OSSL_PACKAGE);\n#endif\n#elif defined(OPENSSL_IS_AWSLC)\n  return msnprintf(buffer, size, \"%s/%s\",\n                   OSSL_PACKAGE,\n                   AWSLC_VERSION_NUMBER_STRING);\n#elif defined(HAVE_OPENSSL_VERSION) && defined(OPENSSL_VERSION_STRING)\n  return msnprintf(buffer, size, \"%s/%s\",\n                   OSSL_PACKAGE, OpenSSL_version(OPENSSL_VERSION_STRING));\n#else\n  /* not LibreSSL, BoringSSL and not using OpenSSL_version */\n\n  char sub[3];\n  unsigned long ssleay_value;\n  sub[2]='\\0';\n  sub[1]='\\0';\n  ssleay_value = OpenSSL_version_num();\n  if(ssleay_value < 0x906000) {\n    ssleay_value = SSLEAY_VERSION_NUMBER;\n    sub[0]='\\0';\n  }\n  else {\n    if(ssleay_value&0xff0) {\n      int minor_ver = (ssleay_value >> 4) & 0xff;\n      if(minor_ver > 26) {\n        /* handle extended version introduced for 0.9.8za */\n        sub[1] = (char) ((minor_ver - 1) % 26 + 'a' + 1);\n        sub[0] = 'z';\n      }\n      else {\n        sub[0] = (char) (minor_ver + 'a' - 1);\n      }\n    }\n    else\n      sub[0]='\\0';\n  }\n\n  return msnprintf(buffer, size, \"%s/%lx.%lx.%lx%s\"\n#ifdef OPENSSL_FIPS\n                   \"-fips\"\n#endif\n                   ,\n                   OSSL_PACKAGE,\n                   (ssleay_value >> 28) & 0xf,\n                   (ssleay_value >> 20) & 0xff,\n                   (ssleay_value >> 12) & 0xff,\n                   sub);\n#endif /* OPENSSL_IS_BORINGSSL */\n}\n\n/* can be called with data == NULL */\nstatic CURLcode ossl_random(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                            unsigned char *entropy, size_t length)\n{\n  int rc;\n  if(data) {\n    if(ossl_seed(data)) /* Initiate the seed if not already done */\n      return CURLE_FAILED_INIT; /* could not seed for some reason */\n  }\n  else {\n    if(!rand_enough())\n      return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\n  }\n  /* RAND_bytes() returns 1 on success, 0 otherwise.  */\n  rc = RAND_bytes(entropy, (ossl_valsize_t)curlx_uztosi(length));\n  return rc == 1 ? CURLE_OK : CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\n}\n\n#if (OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x0090800fL) && !defined(OPENSSL_NO_SHA256)\nstatic CURLcode ossl_sha256sum(const unsigned char *tmp, /* input */\n                               size_t tmplen,\n                               unsigned char *sha256sum /* output */,\n                               size_t unused)\n{\n  EVP_MD_CTX *mdctx;\n  unsigned int len = 0;\n  (void) unused;\n\n  mdctx = EVP_MD_CTX_create();\n  if(!mdctx)\n    return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n  if(!EVP_DigestInit(mdctx, EVP_sha256())) {\n    EVP_MD_CTX_destroy(mdctx);\n    return CURLE_FAILED_INIT;\n  }\n  EVP_DigestUpdate(mdctx, tmp, tmplen);\n  EVP_DigestFinal_ex(mdctx, sha256sum, &len);\n  EVP_MD_CTX_destroy(mdctx);\n  return CURLE_OK;\n}\n#endif\n\nstatic bool ossl_cert_status_request(void)\n{\n#if (OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x0090808fL) && !defined(OPENSSL_NO_TLSEXT) && \\\n  !defined(OPENSSL_NO_OCSP)\n  return TRUE;\n#else\n  return FALSE;\n#endif\n}\n\nstatic void *ossl_get_internals(struct ssl_connect_data *connssl,\n                                CURLINFO info)\n{\n  /* Legacy: CURLINFO_TLS_SESSION must return an SSL_CTX pointer. */\n  struct ossl_ctx *octx = (struct ossl_ctx *)connssl->backend;\n  DEBUGASSERT(octx);\n  return info == CURLINFO_TLS_SESSION ?\n    (void *)octx->ssl_ctx : (void *)octx->ssl;\n}\n\nconst struct Curl_ssl Curl_ssl_openssl = {\n  { CURLSSLBACKEND_OPENSSL, \"openssl\" }, /* info */\n\n  SSLSUPP_CA_PATH |\n  SSLSUPP_CAINFO_BLOB |\n  SSLSUPP_CERTINFO |\n  SSLSUPP_PINNEDPUBKEY |\n  SSLSUPP_SSL_CTX |\n#ifdef HAVE_SSL_CTX_SET_CIPHERSUITES\n  SSLSUPP_TLS13_CIPHERSUITES |\n#endif\n#ifdef USE_ECH_OPENSSL\n  SSLSUPP_ECH |\n#endif\n  SSLSUPP_CA_CACHE |\n  SSLSUPP_HTTPS_PROXY |\n  SSLSUPP_CIPHER_LIST,\n\n  sizeof(struct ossl_ctx),\n\n  ossl_init,                /* init */\n  ossl_cleanup,             /* cleanup */\n  Curl_ossl_version,        /* version */\n  ossl_shutdown,            /* shutdown */\n  ossl_data_pending,        /* data_pending */\n  ossl_random,              /* random */\n  ossl_cert_status_request, /* cert_status_request */\n  ossl_connect,             /* connect */\n  ossl_connect_nonblocking, /* connect_nonblocking */\n  Curl_ssl_adjust_pollset,  /* adjust_pollset */\n  ossl_get_internals,       /* get_internals */\n  ossl_close,               /* close_one */\n  ossl_close_all,           /* close_all */\n  ossl_set_engine,          /* set_engine */\n  ossl_set_engine_default,  /* set_engine_default */\n  ossl_engines_list,        /* engines_list */\n  NULL,                     /* false_start */\n#if (OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x0090800fL) && !defined(OPENSSL_NO_SHA256)\n  ossl_sha256sum,           /* sha256sum */\n#else\n  NULL,                     /* sha256sum */\n#endif\n  ossl_recv,                /* recv decrypted data */\n  ossl_send,                /* send data to encrypt */\n  ossl_get_channel_binding  /* get_channel_binding */\n};\n\n#endif /* USE_OPENSSL */\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        " {",
        "   DEBUGASSERT(0 == encrypting);",

        "+  if(!encrypting && num >= 0) {",
        "     int klen = curlx_uztosi(strlen((char *)global_passwd));",
        "-    memcpy(buf, global_passwd, klen + 1);",
        "-    return klen;",
        "+    if(num > klen) {",
        "+      memcpy(buf, global_passwd, klen + 1);",
        "+      return klen;",
        "+    }",

155. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/lib/vtls/openssl.c",
  "limit": 30,
  "offset": 2355
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "        switch(target) {\n        case GEN_DNS: /* name/pattern comparison */\n          /* The OpenSSL manpage explicitly says: \"In general it cannot be\n             assumed that the data returned by ASN1_STRING_data() is null\n             terminated or does not contain embedded nulls.\" But also that\n             \"The actual format of the data will depend on the actual string\n             type itself: for example for an IA5String the data will be ASCII\"\n\n             It has been however verified that in 0.9.6 and 0.9.7, IA5String\n             is always null-terminated.\n          */\n          if(subj_alt_hostcheck(data, altptr, altlen,\n                                peer->hostname, hostlen,\n                                peer->dispname)) {\n            dnsmatched = TRUE;\n          }\n          break;\n\n        case GEN_IPADD: /* IP address comparison */\n          /* compare alternative IP address if the data chunk is the same size\n             our server IP address is */\n          if((altlen == addrlen) && !memcmp(altptr, &addr, altlen)) {\n            ipmatched = TRUE;\n            infof(data,\n                  \" subjectAltName: host \\\"%s\\\" matched cert's IP address!\",\n                  peer->dispname);\n          }\n          break;\n        }\n      }",
    "filePath": "/workspace/lib/vtls/openssl.c",
    "numLines": 30,
    "startLine": 2355,
    "totalLines": 5533
  },
  "type": "text"
}
156. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/lib/cookie.c",
  "limit": 20,
  "offset": 145
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "   * A lead char of cookie_domain is not '.'.\n   * RFC6265 4.1.2.3. The Domain Attribute says:\n   * For example, if the value of the Domain attribute is\n   * \"example.com\", the user agent will include the cookie in the Cookie\n   * header when making HTTP requests to example.com, www.example.com, and\n   * www.corp.example.com.\n   */\n  if(hostname_len == cookie_domain_len)\n    return TRUE;\n  if('.' == *(hostname + hostname_len - cookie_domain_len))\n    return TRUE;\n  return FALSE;\n}\n\n/*\n * matching cookie path and URL path\n * RFC6265 5.1.4 Paths and Path-Match\n */\nstatic bool pathmatch(const char *cookie_path, const char *request_uri)\n{",
    "filePath": "/workspace/lib/cookie.c",
    "numLines": 20,
    "startLine": 145,
    "totalLines": 1751
  },
  "type": "text"
}

  "new_string": "          if((altlen == strlen(altptr)) &&\n             /* if this is not true, there was an embedded zero in the name\n                string and we cannot match it. */\n             subj_alt_hostcheck(data, altptr, altlen,\n                                peer->hostname, hostlen,\n                                peer->dispname)) {\n            dnsmatched = TRUE;\n          }",
  "old_string": "          if(subj_alt_hostcheck(data, altptr, altlen,\n                                peer->hostname, hostlen,\n                                peer->dispname)) {\n            dnsmatched = TRUE;\n          }",

No code\n * but vtls.c should ever call or use these functions.\n */\n\n#include \"curl_setup.h\"\n\n#if defined(USE_QUICHE) || defined(USE_OPENSSL)\n\n#include <limits.h>\n\n/* Wincrypt must be included before anything that could include OpenSSL. */\n#if defined(USE_WIN32_CRYPTO)\n#include <wincrypt.h>\n/* Undefine wincrypt conflicting symbols for BoringSSL. */\n#undef X509_NAME\n#undef X509_EXTENSIONS\n#undef PKCS7_ISSUER_AND_SERIAL\n#undef PKCS7_SIGNER_INFO\n#undef OCSP_REQUEST\n#undef OCSP_RESPONSE\n#endif\n\n#include \"urldata.h\"\n#include \"sendf.h\"\n#include \"formdata.h\" /* for the boundary function */\n#include \"url.h\" /* for the ssl config check function */\n#include \"inet_pton.h\"\n#include \"openssl.h\"\n#include \"connect.h\"\n#include \"slist.h\"\n#include \"select.h\"\n#include \"vtls.h\"\n#include \"vtls_int.h\"\n#include \"vtls_scache.h\"\n#include \"vauth/vauth.h\"\n#include \"keylog.h\"\n#include \"strcase.h\"\n#include \"hostcheck.h\"\n#include \"multiif.h\"\n#include \"strerror.h\"\n#include \"curl_printf.h\"\n\n#include <openssl/ssl.h>\n#include <openssl/rand.h>\n#include <openssl/x509v3.h>\n#ifndef OPENSSL_NO_DSA\n#include <openssl/dsa.h>\n#endif\n#include <openssl/dh.h>\n#include <openssl/err.h>\n#include <openssl/md5.h>\n#include <openssl/conf.h>\n#include <openssl/bn.h>\n#include <openssl/rsa.h>\n#include <openssl/bio.h>\n#include <openssl/buffer.h>\n#include <openssl/pkcs12.h>\n#include <openssl/tls1.h>\n#include <openssl/evp.h>\n\n#if defined(HAVE_SSL_SET1_ECH_CONFIG_LIST)\n#define USE_ECH_OPENSSL\n#endif\n\n#ifdef USE_ECH_OPENSSL\n# if !defined(OPENSSL_IS_BORINGSSL) && !defined(OPENSSL_IS_AWSLC)\n#  include <openssl/ech.h>\n# endif\n#endif /* USE_ECH_OPENSSL */\n\n#if (OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x0090808fL) && !defined(OPENSSL_NO_OCSP)\n#include <openssl/ocsp.h>\n#endif\n\n#if (OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x0090700fL) && /* 0.9.7 or later */     \\\n  !defined(OPENSSL_NO_ENGINE) && !defined(OPENSSL_NO_UI_CONSOLE)\n#define USE_OPENSSL_ENGINE\n#include <openssl/engine.h>\n#endif\n\n#if OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x03000000fL && !defined(OPENSSL_NO_UI_CONSOLE)\n#include <openssl/provider.h>\n#include <openssl/store.h>\n/* this is used in the following conditions to make them easier to read */\n#define OPENSSL_HAS_PROVIDERS\n#endif\n\n#include \"warnless.h\"\n\n/* The last #include files should be: */\n#include \"curl_memory.h\"\n#include \"memdebug.h\"\n\n#ifndef ARRAYSIZE\n#define ARRAYSIZE(A) (sizeof(A)/sizeof((A)[0]))\n#endif\n\n/* Uncomment the ALLOW_RENEG line to a real #define if you want to allow TLS\n   renegotiations when built with BoringSSL. Renegotiating is non-compliant\n   with HTTP/2 and \"an extremely dangerous protocol feature\". Beware.\n\n#define ALLOW_RENEG 1\n */\n\n#ifndef OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER\n#error \"OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER not defined\"\n#endif\n\n#if defined(USE_OPENSSL_ENGINE) || defined(OPENSSL_HAS_PROVIDERS)\n#include <openssl/ui.h>\n#endif\n\n#if OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x00909000L\n#define SSL_METHOD_QUAL const\n#else\n#define SSL_METHOD_QUAL\n#endif\n\n#if (OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x10000000L)\n#define HAVE_ERR_REMOVE_THREAD_STATE 1\n#endif\n\n#if (OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x10100000L) && /* OpenSSL 1.1.0+ */ \\\n    !(defined(LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER) && \\\n      LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER < 0x20700000L)\n#define SSLEAY_VERSION_NUMBER OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER\n#define HAVE_X509_GET0_EXTENSIONS 1 /* added in 1.1.0 -pre1 */\n#define HAVE_OPAQUE_EVP_PKEY 1 /* since 1.1.0 -pre3 */\n#define HAVE_OPAQUE_RSA_DSA_DH 1 /* since 1.1.0 -pre5 */\n#define CONST_EXTS const\n#define HAVE_ERR_REMOVE_THREAD_STATE_DEPRECATED 1\n\n/* funny typecast define due to difference in API */\n#ifdef LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER\n#define ARG2_X509_signature_print (X509_ALGOR *)\n#else\n#define ARG2_X509_signature_print\n#endif\n\n#else\n/* For OpenSSL before 1.1.0 */\n#define ASN1_STRING_get0_data(x) ASN1_STRING_data(x)\n#define X509_get0_notBefore(x) X509_get_notBefore(x)\n#define X509_get0_notAfter(x) X509_get_notAfter(x)\n#define CONST_EXTS /* nope */\n#ifndef LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER\n#define OpenSSL_version_num() SSLeay()\n#endif\n#endif\n\n#if (OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x1000200fL) && /* 1.0.2 or later */ \\\n    !(defined(LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER) && \\\n      LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER < 0x20700000L)\n#define HAVE_X509_GET0_SIGNATURE 1\n#endif\n\n#if OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x10002003L && \\\n  OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER <= 0x10002FFFL && \\\n  !defined(OPENSSL_NO_COMP)\n#define HAVE_SSL_COMP_FREE_COMPRESSION_METHODS 1\n#endif\n\n#if (OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER < 0x0090808fL)\n/* not present in older OpenSSL */\n#define OPENSSL_load_builtin_modules(x)\n#endif\n\n#if (OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x30000000L)\n#define HAVE_EVP_PKEY_GET_PARAMS 1\n#endif\n\n#ifdef HAVE_EVP_PKEY_GET_PARAMS\n#include <openssl/core_names.h>\n#define DECLARE_PKEY_PARAM_BIGNUM(name) BIGNUM *name = NULL\n#define FREE_PKEY_PARAM_BIGNUM(name) BN_clear_free(name)\n#else\n#define DECLARE_PKEY_PARAM_BIGNUM(name) const BIGNUM *name\n#define FREE_PKEY_PARAM_BIGNUM(name)\n#endif\n\n/* Whether SSL_CTX_set_ciphersuites is available.\n * OpenSSL: supported since 1.1.1 (commit a53b5be6a05)\n * BoringSSL: no\n * LibreSSL: supported since 3.4.1 (released 2021-10-14)\n */\n#if ((OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x10101000L && \\\n      !defined(LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER)) || \\\n     (defined(LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER) && \\\n      LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x3040100fL)) && \\\n    !defined(OPENSSL_IS_BORINGSSL)\n  #define HAVE_SSL_CTX_SET_CIPHERSUITES\n  #if !defined(OPENSSL_IS_AWSLC)\n    #define HAVE_SSL_CTX_SET_POST_HANDSHAKE_AUTH\n  #endif\n#endif\n\n/*\n * Whether SSL_CTX_set1_curves_list is available.\n * OpenSSL: supported since 1.0.2, see\n *   https://docs.openssl.org/master/man3/SSL_CTX_set1_curves/\n * BoringSSL: supported since 5fd1807d95f7 (committed 2016-09-30)\n * LibreSSL: since 2.5.3 (April 12, 2017)\n */\n#if (OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x10002000L) ||  \\\n  defined(OPENSSL_IS_BORINGSSL)\n#define HAVE_SSL_CTX_SET_EC_CURVES\n#endif\n\n#if defined(LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER)\n#define OSSL_PACKAGE \"LibreSSL\"\n#elif defined(OPENSSL_IS_BORINGSSL)\n#define OSSL_PACKAGE \"BoringSSL\"\n#elif defined(OPENSSL_IS_AWSLC)\n#define OSSL_PACKAGE \"AWS-LC\"\n#else\n# if (defined(USE_NGTCP2) && defined(USE_NGHTTP3)) || defined(USE_MSH3)\n#   define OSSL_PACKAGE \"quictls\"\n# else\n#   define OSSL_PACKAGE \"OpenSSL\"\n#endif\n#endif\n\n#if defined(OPENSSL_IS_BORINGSSL) || defined(OPENSSL_IS_AWSLC)\ntypedef size_t numcert_t;\n#else\ntypedef int numcert_t;\n#endif\n#define ossl_valsize_t numcert_t\n\n#if (OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x10100000L)\n/* up2date versions of OpenSSL maintain reasonably secure defaults without\n * breaking compatibility, so it is better not to override the defaults in curl\n */\n#define DEFAULT_CIPHER_SELECTION NULL\n#else\n/* not the case with old versions of OpenSSL */\n#define DEFAULT_CIPHER_SELECTION \\\n  \"ALL:!EXPORT:!EXPORT40:!EXPORT56:!aNULL:!LOW:!RC4:@STRENGTH\"\n#endif\n\n#ifdef HAVE_OPENSSL_SRP\n/* the function exists */\n#ifdef USE_TLS_SRP\n/* the functionality is not disabled */\n#define USE_OPENSSL_SRP\n#endif\n#endif\n\n#if (OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x10100000L)\n#define HAVE_RANDOM_INIT_BY_DEFAULT 1\n#endif\n\n#if (OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x10100000L) && \\\n    !(defined(LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER) && \\\n      LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER < 0x2070100fL) && \\\n    !defined(OPENSSL_IS_BORINGSSL) && \\\n    !defined(OPENSSL_IS_AWSLC)\n#define HAVE_OPENSSL_VERSION\n#endif\n\n#if defined(OPENSSL_IS_BORINGSSL) || defined(OPENSSL_IS_AWSLC)\ntypedef uint32_t sslerr_t;\n#else\ntypedef unsigned long sslerr_t;\n#endif\n\n/*\n * Whether the OpenSSL version has the API needed to support sharing an\n * X509_STORE between connections. The API is:\n * * `X509_STORE_up_ref`       -- Introduced: OpenSSL 1.1.0.\n */\n#if (OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x10100000L) /* OpenSSL >= 1.1.0 */\n#define HAVE_SSL_X509_STORE_SHARE\n#endif\n\n/* What API version do we use? */\n#if defined(LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER)\n#define USE_PRE_1_1_API (LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER < 0x2070000f)\n#else /* !LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER */\n#define USE_PRE_1_1_API (OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER < 0x10100000L)\n#endif /* !LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER */\n\nstatic CURLcode ossl_certchain(struct Curl_easy *data, SSL *ssl);\n\nstatic CURLcode push_certinfo(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                              BIO *mem, const char *label, int num)\n  WARN_UNUSED_RESULT;\n\nstatic CURLcode push_certinfo(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                              BIO *mem, const char *label, int num)\n{\n  char *ptr;\n  long len = BIO_get_mem_data(mem, &ptr);\n  CURLcode result = Curl_ssl_push_certinfo_len(data, num, label, ptr, len);\n  (void)BIO_reset(mem);\n  return result;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode pubkey_show(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                            BIO *mem,\n                            int num,\n                            const char *type,\n                            const char *name,\n                            const BIGNUM *bn)\n{\n  char namebuf[32];\n\n  msnprintf(namebuf, sizeof(namebuf), \"%s(%s)\", type, name);\n\n  if(bn)\n    BN_print(mem, bn);\n  return push_certinfo(data, mem, namebuf, num);\n}\n\n#ifdef HAVE_OPAQUE_RSA_DSA_DH\n#define print_pubkey_BN(_type, _name, _num)              \\\n  pubkey_show(data, mem, _num, #_type, #_name, _name)\n\n#else\n#define print_pubkey_BN(_type, _name, _num)    \\\ndo {                              \\\n  if(_type->_name) { \\\n    pubkey_show(data, mem, _num, #_type, #_name, _type->_name); \\\n  } \\\n} while(0)\n#endif\n\nstatic int asn1_object_dump(ASN1_OBJECT *a, char *buf, size_t len)\n{\n  int i, ilen;\n\n  ilen = (int)len;\n  if(ilen < 0)\n    return 1; /* buffer too big */\n\n  i = i2t_ASN1_OBJECT(buf, ilen, a);\n\n  if(i >= ilen)\n    return 1; /* buffer too small */\n\n  return 0;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode X509V3_ext(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                           int certnum,\n                           CONST_EXTS STACK_OF(X509_EXTENSION) *exts)\n{\n  int i;\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OK;\n\n  if((int)sk_X509_EXTENSION_num(exts) <= 0)\n    /* no extensions, bail out */\n    return result;\n\n  for(i = 0; i < (int)sk_X509_EXTENSION_num(exts); i++) {\n    ASN1_OBJECT *obj;\n    X509_EXTENSION *ext = sk_X509_EXTENSION_value(exts, (ossl_valsize_t)i);\n    BUF_MEM *biomem;\n    char namebuf[128];\n    BIO *bio_out = BIO_new(BIO_s_mem());\n\n    if(!bio_out)\n      return result;\n\n    obj = X509_EXTENSION_get_object(ext);\n\n    asn1_object_dump(obj, namebuf, sizeof(namebuf));\n\n    if(!X509V3_EXT_print(bio_out, ext, 0, 0))\n      ASN1_STRING_print(bio_out, (ASN1_STRING *)X509_EXTENSION_get_data(ext));\n\n    BIO_get_mem_ptr(bio_out, &biomem);\n    result = Curl_ssl_push_certinfo_len(data, certnum, namebuf, biomem->data,\n                                        biomem->length);\n    BIO_free(bio_out);\n    if(result)\n      break;\n  }\n  return result;\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode ossl_certchain(struct Curl_easy *data, SSL *ssl)\n{\n  CURLcode result;\n  STACK_OF(X509) *sk;\n  int i;\n  numcert_t numcerts;\n  BIO *mem;\n\n  DEBUGASSERT(ssl);\n\n  sk = SSL_get_peer_cert_chain(ssl);\n  if(!sk) {\n    return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n  }\n\n  numcerts = sk_X509_num(sk);\n\n  result = Curl_ssl_init_certinfo(data, (int)numcerts);\n  if(result)\n    return result;\n\n  mem = BIO_new(BIO_s_mem());\n  if(!mem)\n    result = CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n\n  for(i = 0; !result && (i < (int)numcerts); i++) {\n    ASN1_INTEGER *num;\n    X509 *x = sk_X509_value(sk, (ossl_valsize_t)i);\n    EVP_PKEY *pubkey = NULL;\n    int j;\n    const ASN1_BIT_STRING *psig = NULL;\n\n    X509_NAME_print_ex(mem, X509_get_subject_name(x), 0, XN_FLAG_ONELINE);\n    result = push_certinfo(data, mem, \"Subject\", i);\n    if(result)\n      break;\n\n    X509_NAME_print_ex(mem, X509_get_issuer_name(x), 0, XN_FLAG_ONELINE);\n    result = push_certinfo(data, mem, \"Issuer\", i);\n    if(result)\n      break;\n\n    BIO_printf(mem, \"%lx\", X509_get_version(x));\n    result = push_certinfo(data, mem, \"Version\", i);\n    if(result)\n      break;\n\n    num = X509_get_serialNumber(x);\n    if(num->type == V_ASN1_NEG_INTEGER)\n      BIO_puts(mem, \"-\");\n    for(j = 0; j < num->length; j++)\n      BIO_printf(mem, \"%02x\", num->data[j]);\n    result = push_certinfo(data, mem, \"Serial Number\", i);\n    if(result)\n      break;\n\n#if defined(HAVE_X509_GET0_SIGNATURE) && defined(HAVE_X509_GET0_EXTENSIONS)\n    {\n      const X509_ALGOR *sigalg = NULL;\n      X509_PUBKEY *xpubkey = NULL;\n      ASN1_OBJECT *pubkeyoid = NULL;\n\n      X509_get0_signature(&psig, &sigalg, x);\n      if(sigalg) {\n        const ASN1_OBJECT *sigalgoid = NULL;\n        X509_ALGOR_get0(&sigalgoid, NULL, NULL, sigalg);\n        i2a_ASN1_OBJECT(mem, sigalgoid);\n        result = push_certinfo(data, mem, \"Signature Algorithm\", i);\n        if(result)\n          break;\n      }\n\n      xpubkey = X509_get_X509_PUBKEY(x);\n      if(xpubkey) {\n        X509_PUBKEY_get0_param(&pubkeyoid, NULL, NULL, NULL, xpubkey);\n        if(pubkeyoid) {\n          i2a_ASN1_OBJECT(mem, pubkeyoid);\n          result = push_certinfo(data, mem, \"Public Key Algorithm\", i);\n          if(result)\n            break;\n        }\n      }\n\n      result = X509V3_ext(data, i, X509_get0_extensions(x));\n      if(result)\n        break;\n    }\n#else\n    {\n      /* before OpenSSL 1.0.2 */\n      X509_CINF *cinf = x->cert_info;\n\n      i2a_ASN1_OBJECT(mem, cinf->signature->algorithm);\n      result = push_certinfo(data, mem, \"Signature Algorithm\", i);\n\n      if(!result) {\n        i2a_ASN1_OBJECT(mem, cinf->key->algor->algorithm);\n        result = push_certinfo(data, mem, \"Public Key Algorithm\", i);\n      }\n\n      if(!result)\n        result = X509V3_ext(data, i, cinf->extensions);\n\n      if(result)\n        break;\n\n      psig = x->signature;\n    }\n#endif\n\n    ASN1_TIME_print(mem, X509_get0_notBefore(x));\n    result = push_certinfo(data, mem, \"Start date\", i);\n    if(result)\n      break;\n\n    ASN1_TIME_print(mem, X509_get0_notAfter(x));\n    result = push_certinfo(data, mem, \"Expire date\", i);\n    if(result)\n      break;\n\n    pubkey = X509_get_pubkey(x);\n    if(!pubkey)\n      infof(data, \"   Unable to load public key\");\n    else {\n      int pktype;\n#ifdef HAVE_OPAQUE_EVP_PKEY\n      pktype = EVP_PKEY_id(pubkey);\n#else\n      pktype = pubkey->type;\n#endif\n      switch(pktype) {\n      case EVP_PKEY_RSA: {\n#ifndef HAVE_EVP_PKEY_GET_PARAMS\n        RSA *rsa;\n#ifdef HAVE_OPAQUE_EVP_PKEY\n        rsa = EVP_PKEY_get0_RSA(pubkey);\n#else\n        rsa = pubkey->pkey.rsa;\n#endif /* HAVE_OPAQUE_EVP_PKEY */\n#endif /* !HAVE_EVP_PKEY_GET_PARAMS */\n\n        {\n#ifdef HAVE_OPAQUE_RSA_DSA_DH\n          DECLARE_PKEY_PARAM_BIGNUM(n);\n          DECLARE_PKEY_PARAM_BIGNUM(e);\n#ifdef HAVE_EVP_PKEY_GET_PARAMS\n          EVP_PKEY_get_bn_param(pubkey, OSSL_PKEY_PARAM_RSA_N, &n);\n          EVP_PKEY_get_bn_param(pubkey, OSSL_PKEY_PARAM_RSA_E, &e);\n#else\n          RSA_get0_key(rsa, &n, &e, NULL);\n#endif /* HAVE_EVP_PKEY_GET_PARAMS */\n          BIO_printf(mem, \"%d\", n ? BN_num_bits(n) : 0);\n#else\n          BIO_printf(mem, \"%d\", rsa->n ? BN_num_bits(rsa->n) : 0);\n#endif /* HAVE_OPAQUE_RSA_DSA_DH */\n          result = push_certinfo(data, mem, \"RSA Public Key\", i);\n          if(result)\n            break;\n          print_pubkey_BN(rsa, n, i);\n          print_pubkey_BN(rsa, e, i);\n          FREE_PKEY_PARAM_BIGNUM(n);\n          FREE_PKEY_PARAM_BIGNUM(e);\n        }\n\n        break;\n      }\n      case EVP_PKEY_DSA:\n      {\n#ifndef OPENSSL_NO_DSA\n#ifndef HAVE_EVP_PKEY_GET_PARAMS\n        DSA *dsa;\n#ifdef HAVE_OPAQUE_EVP_PKEY\n        dsa = EVP_PKEY_get0_DSA(pubkey);\n#else\n        dsa = pubkey->pkey.dsa;\n#endif /* HAVE_OPAQUE_EVP_PKEY */\n#endif /* !HAVE_EVP_PKEY_GET_PARAMS */\n        {\n#ifdef HAVE_OPAQUE_RSA_DSA_DH\n          DECLARE_PKEY_PARAM_BIGNUM(p);\n          DECLARE_PKEY_PARAM_BIGNUM(q);\n          DECLARE_PKEY_PARAM_BIGNUM(g);\n          DECLARE_PKEY_PARAM_BIGNUM(pub_key);\n#ifdef HAVE_EVP_PKEY_GET_PARAMS\n          EVP_PKEY_get_bn_param(pubkey, OSSL_PKEY_PARAM_FFC_P, &p);\n          EVP_PKEY_get_bn_param(pubkey, OSSL_PKEY_PARAM_FFC_Q, &q);\n          EVP_PKEY_get_bn_param(pubkey, OSSL_PKEY_PARAM_FFC_G, &g);\n          EVP_PKEY_get_bn_param(pubkey, OSSL_PKEY_PARAM_PUB_KEY, &pub_key);\n#else\n          DSA_get0_pqg(dsa, &p, &q, &g);\n          DSA_get0_key(dsa, &pub_key, NULL);\n#endif /* HAVE_EVP_PKEY_GET_PARAMS */\n#endif /* HAVE_OPAQUE_RSA_DSA_DH */\n          print_pubkey_BN(dsa, p, i);\n          print_pubkey_BN(dsa, q, i);\n          print_pubkey_BN(dsa, g, i);\n          print_pubkey_BN(dsa, pub_key, i);\n          FREE_PKEY_PARAM_BIGNUM(p);\n          FREE_PKEY_PARAM_BIGNUM(q);\n          FREE_PKEY_PARAM_BIGNUM(g);\n          FREE_PKEY_PARAM_BIGNUM(pub_key);\n        }\n#endif /* !OPENSSL_NO_DSA */\n        break;\n      }\n      case EVP_PKEY_DH: {\n#ifndef HAVE_EVP_PKEY_GET_PARAMS\n        DH *dh;\n#ifdef HAVE_OPAQUE_EVP_PKEY\n        dh = EVP_PKEY_get0_DH(pubkey);\n#else\n        dh = pubkey->pkey.dh;\n#endif /* HAVE_OPAQUE_EVP_PKEY */\n#endif /* !HAVE_EVP_PKEY_GET_PARAMS */\n        {\n#ifdef HAVE_OPAQUE_RSA_DSA_DH\n          DECLARE_PKEY_PARAM_BIGNUM(p);\n          DECLARE_PKEY_PARAM_BIGNUM(q);\n          DECLARE_PKEY_PARAM_BIGNUM(g);\n          DECLARE_PKEY_PARAM_BIGNUM(pub_key);\n#ifdef HAVE_EVP_PKEY_GET_PARAMS\n          EVP_PKEY_get_bn_param(pubkey, OSSL_PKEY_PARAM_FFC_P, &p);\n          EVP_PKEY_get_bn_param(pubkey, OSSL_PKEY_PARAM_FFC_Q, &q);\n          EVP_PKEY_get_bn_param(pubkey, OSSL_PKEY_PARAM_FFC_G, &g);\n          EVP_PKEY_get_bn_param(pubkey, OSSL_PKEY_PARAM_PUB_KEY, &pub_key);\n#else\n          DH_get0_pqg(dh, &p, &q, &g);\n          DH_get0_key(dh, &pub_key, NULL);\n#endif /* HAVE_EVP_PKEY_GET_PARAMS */\n          print_pubkey_BN(dh, p, i);\n          print_pubkey_BN(dh, q, i);\n          print_pubkey_BN(dh, g, i);\n#else\n          print_pubkey_BN(dh, p, i);\n          print_pubkey_BN(dh, g, i);\n#endif /* HAVE_OPAQUE_RSA_DSA_DH */\n          print_pubkey_BN(dh, pub_key, i);\n          FREE_PKEY_PARAM_BIGNUM(p);\n          FREE_PKEY_PARAM_BIGNUM(q);\n          FREE_PKEY_PARAM_BIGNUM(g);\n          FREE_PKEY_PARAM_BIGNUM(pub_key);\n        }\n        break;\n      }\n      }\n      EVP_PKEY_free(pubkey);\n    }\n\n    if(!result && psig) {\n      for(j = 0; j < psig->length; j++)\n        BIO_printf(mem, \"%02x:\", psig->data[j]);\n      result = push_certinfo(data, mem, \"Signature\", i);\n    }\n\n    if(!result) {\n      PEM_write_bio_X509(mem, x);\n      result = push_certinfo(data, mem, \"Cert\", i);\n    }\n  }\n\n  BIO_free(mem);\n\n  if(result)\n    /* cleanup all leftovers */\n    Curl_ssl_free_certinfo(data);\n\n  return result;\n}\n\n#endif /* quiche or OpenSSL */\n\n#ifdef USE_OPENSSL\n\n#if USE_PRE_1_1_API\n#if !defined(LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER) || LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER < 0x2070000fL\n#define BIO_set_init(x,v)          ((x)->init=(v))\n#define BIO_get_data(x)            ((x)->ptr)\n#define BIO_set_data(x,v)          ((x)->ptr=(v))\n#endif\n#define BIO_get_shutdown(x)        ((x)->shutdown)\n#define BIO_set_shutdown(x,v)      ((x)->shutdown=(v))\n#endif /* USE_PRE_1_1_API */\n\nstatic int ossl_bio_cf_create(BIO *bio)\n{\n  BIO_set_shutdown(bio, 1);\n  BIO_set_init(bio, 1);\n#if USE_PRE_1_1_API\n  bio->num = -1;\n#endif\n  BIO_set_data(bio, NULL);\n  return 1;\n}\n\nstatic int ossl_bio_cf_destroy(BIO *bio)\n{\n  if(!bio)\n    return 0;\n  return 1;\n}\n\nstatic long ossl_bio_cf_ctrl(BIO *bio, int cmd, long num, void *ptr)\n{\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf = BIO_get_data(bio);\n  long ret = 1;\n\n  (void)cf;\n  (void)ptr;\n  switch(cmd) {\n  case BIO_CTRL_GET_CLOSE:\n    ret = (long)BIO_get_shutdown(bio);\n    break;\n  case BIO_CTRL_SET_CLOSE:\n    BIO_set_shutdown(bio, (int)num);\n    break;\n  case BIO_CTRL_FLUSH:\n    /* we do no delayed writes, but if we ever would, this\n     * needs to trigger it. */\n    ret = 1;\n    break;\n  case BIO_CTRL_DUP:\n    ret = 1;\n    break;\n#ifdef BIO_CTRL_EOF\n  case BIO_CTRL_EOF:\n    /* EOF has been reached on input? */\n    return !cf->next || !cf->next->connected;\n#endif\n  default:\n    ret = 0;\n    break;\n  }\n  return ret;\n}\n\nstatic int ossl_bio_cf_out_write(BIO *bio, const char *buf, int blen)\n{\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf = BIO_get_data(bio);\n  struct ssl_connect_data *connssl = cf->ctx;\n  struct ossl_ctx *octx = (struct ossl_ctx *)connssl->backend;\n  struct Curl_easy *data = CF_DATA_CURRENT(cf);\n  ssize_t nwritten;\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_SEND_ERROR;\n\n  DEBUGASSERT(data);\n  if(blen < 0)\n    return 0;\n\n  nwritten = Curl_conn_cf_send(cf->next, data, buf, (size_t)blen, FALSE,\n                               &result);\n  CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"ossl_bio_cf_out_write(len=%d) -> %d, err=%d\",\n              blen, (int)nwritten, result);\n  BIO_clear_retry_flags(bio);\n  octx->io_result = result;\n  if(nwritten < 0) {\n    if(CURLE_AGAIN == result)\n      BIO_set_retry_write(bio);\n  }\n  return (int)nwritten;\n}\n\nstatic int ossl_bio_cf_in_read(BIO *bio, char *buf, int blen)\n{\n  struct Curl_cfilter *cf = BIO_get_data(bio);\n  struct ssl_connect_data *connssl = cf->ctx;\n  struct ossl_ctx *octx = (struct ossl_ctx *)connssl->backend;\n  struct Curl_easy *data = CF_DATA_CURRENT(cf);\n  ssize_t nread;\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_RECV_ERROR;\n\n  DEBUGASSERT(data);\n  /* OpenSSL catches this case, so should we. */\n  if(!buf)\n    return 0;\n  if(blen < 0)\n    return 0;\n\n  nread = Curl_conn_cf_recv(cf->next, data, buf, (size_t)blen, &result);\n  CURL_TRC_CF(data, cf, \"ossl_bio_cf_in_read(len=%d) -> %d, err=%d\",\n              blen, (int)nread, result);\n  BIO_clear_retry_flags(bio);\n  octx->io_result = result;\n  if(nread < 0) {\n    if(CURLE_AGAIN == result)\n      BIO_set_retry_read(bio);\n  }\n  else if(nread == 0) {\n    connssl->peer_closed = TRUE;\n  }\n\n  /* Before returning server replies to the SSL instance, we need\n   * to have setup the x509 store or verification will fail. */\n  if(!octx->x509_store_setup) {\n    result = Curl_ssl_setup_x509_store(cf, data, octx->ssl_ctx);\n    if(result) {\n      octx->io_result = result;\n      return -1;\n    }\n    octx->x509_store_setup = TRUE;\n  }\n\n  return (int)nread;\n}\n\n#if USE_PRE_1_1_API\n\nstatic BIO_METHOD ossl_bio_cf_meth_1_0 = {\n  BIO_TYPE_MEM,\n  \"OpenSSL CF BIO\",\n  ossl_bio_cf_out_write,\n  ossl_bio_cf_in_read,\n  NULL,                    /* puts is never called */\n  NULL,                    /* gets is never called */\n  ossl_bio_cf_ctrl,\n  ossl_bio_cf_create,\n  ossl_bio_cf_destroy,\n  NULL\n};\n\nstatic BIO_METHOD *ossl_bio_cf_method_create(void)\n{\n  return &ossl_bio_cf_meth_1_0;\n}\n\n#define ossl_bio_cf_method_free(m) Curl_nop_stmt\n\n#else\n\nstatic BIO_METHOD *ossl_bio_cf_method_create(void)\n{\n  BIO_METHOD *m = BIO_meth_new(BIO_TYPE_MEM, \"OpenSSL CF BIO\");\n  if(m) {\n    BIO_meth_set_write(m, &ossl_bio_cf_out_write);\n    BIO_meth_set_read(m, &ossl_bio_cf_in_read);\n    BIO_meth_set_ctrl(m, &ossl_bio_cf_ctrl);\n    BIO_meth_set_create(m, &ossl_bio_cf_create);\n    BIO_meth_set_destroy(m, &ossl_bio_cf_destroy);\n  }\n  return m;\n}\n\nstatic void ossl_bio_cf_method_free(BIO_METHOD *m)\n{\n  if(m)\n    BIO_meth_free(m);\n}\n\n#endif\n\n\n/*\n * Number of bytes to read from the random number seed file. This must be\n * a finite value (because some entropy \"files\" like /dev/urandom have\n * an infinite length), but must be large enough to provide enough\n * entropy to properly seed OpenSSL's PRNG.\n */\n#define RAND_LOAD_LENGTH 1024\n\n#ifdef HAVE_KEYLOG_CALLBACK\nstatic void ossl_keylog_callback(const SSL *ssl, const char *line)\n{\n  (void)ssl;\n\n  Curl_tls_keylog_write_line(line);\n}\n#else\n/*\n * ossl_log_tls12_secret is called by libcurl to make the CLIENT_RANDOMs if the\n * OpenSSL being used does not have native support for doing that.\n */\nstatic void\nossl_log_tls12_secret(const SSL *ssl, bool *keylog_done)\n{\n  const SSL_SESSION *session = SSL_get_session(ssl);\n  unsigned char client_random[SSL3_RANDOM_SIZE];\n  unsigned char master_key[SSL_MAX_MASTER_KEY_LENGTH];\n  int master_key_length = 0;\n\n  if(!session || *keylog_done)\n    return;\n\n#if OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x10100000L &&    \\\n  !(defined(LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER) &&         \\\n    LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER < 0x20700000L)\n  /* ssl->s3 is not checked in OpenSSL 1.1.0-pre6, but let's assume that\n   * we have a valid SSL context if we have a non-NULL session. */\n  SSL_get_client_random(ssl, client_random, SSL3_RANDOM_SIZE);\n  master_key_length = (int)\n    SSL_SESSION_get_master_key(session, master_key, SSL_MAX_MASTER_KEY_LENGTH);\n#else\n  if(ssl->s3 && session->master_key_length > 0) {\n    master_key_length = session->master_key_length;\n    memcpy(master_key, session->master_key, session->master_key_length);\n    memcpy(client_random, ssl->s3->client_random, SSL3_RANDOM_SIZE);\n  }\n#endif\n\n  /* The handshake has not progressed sufficiently yet, or this is a TLS 1.3\n   * session (when curl was built with older OpenSSL headers and running with\n   * newer OpenSSL runtime libraries). */\n  if(master_key_length <= 0)\n    return;\n\n  *keylog_done = TRUE;\n  Curl_tls_keylog_write(\"CLIENT_RANDOM\", client_random,\n                        master_key, master_key_length);\n}\n#endif /* !HAVE_KEYLOG_CALLBACK */\n\nstatic const char *SSL_ERROR_to_str(int err)\n{\n  switch(err) {\n  case SSL_ERROR_NONE:\n    return \"SSL_ERROR_NONE\";\n  case SSL_ERROR_SSL:\n    return \"SSL_ERROR_SSL\";\n  case SSL_ERROR_WANT_READ:\n    return \"SSL_ERROR_WANT_READ\";\n  case SSL_ERROR_WANT_WRITE:\n    return \"SSL_ERROR_WANT_WRITE\";\n  case SSL_ERROR_WANT_X509_LOOKUP:\n    return \"SSL_ERROR_WANT_X509_LOOKUP\";\n  case SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL:\n    return \"SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL\";\n  case SSL_ERROR_ZERO_RETURN:\n    return \"SSL_ERROR_ZERO_RETURN\";\n  case SSL_ERROR_WANT_CONNECT:\n    return \"SSL_ERROR_WANT_CONNECT\";\n  case SSL_ERROR_WANT_ACCEPT:\n    return \"SSL_ERROR_WANT_ACCEPT\";\n#if defined(SSL_ERROR_WANT_ASYNC)\n  case SSL_ERROR_WANT_ASYNC:\n    return \"SSL_ERROR_WANT_ASYNC\";\n#endif\n#if defined(SSL_ERROR_WANT_ASYNC_JOB)\n  case SSL_ERROR_WANT_ASYNC_JOB:\n    return \"SSL_ERROR_WANT_ASYNC_JOB\";\n#endif\n#if defined(SSL_ERROR_WANT_EARLY)\n  case SSL_ERROR_WANT_EARLY:\n    return \"SSL_ERROR_WANT_EARLY\";\n#endif\n  default:\n    return \"SSL_ERROR unknown\";\n  }\n}\n\n/* Return error string for last OpenSSL error\n */\nstatic char *ossl_strerror(unsigned long error, char *buf, size_t size)\n{\n  size_t len;\n  DEBUGASSERT(size);\n  *buf = '\\0';\n\n  len = Curl_ossl_version(buf, size);\n  DEBUGASSERT(len < (size - 2));\n  if(len < (size - 2)) {\n    buf += len;\n    size -= (len + 2);\n    *buf++ = ':';\n    *buf++ = ' ';\n    *buf = '\\0';\n  }\n\n#if defined(OPENSSL_IS_BORINGSSL) || defined(OPENSSL_IS_AWSLC)\n  ERR_error_string_n((uint32_t)error, buf, size);\n#else\n  ERR_error_string_n(error, buf, size);\n#endif\n\n  if(!*buf) {\n    const char *msg = error ? \"Unknown error\" : \"No error\";\n    if(strlen(msg) < size)\n      strcpy(buf, msg);\n  }\n\n  return buf;\n}\n\nstatic int passwd_callback(char *buf, int num, int encrypting,\n                           void *global_passwd)\n{\n  DEBUGASSERT(0 == encrypting);\n\n  if(!encrypting && num >= 0) {\n    int klen = curlx_uztosi(strlen((char *)global_passwd));\n    if(num > klen) {\n      memcpy(buf, global_passwd, klen + 1);\n      return klen;\n    }\n  }\n  return 0;\n}\n\n/*\n * rand_enough() returns TRUE if we have seeded the random engine properly.\n */\nstatic bool rand_enough(void)\n{\n  return 0 != RAND_status();\n}\n\nstatic CURLcode ossl_seed(struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n  /* This might get called before it has been added to a multi handle */\n  if(data->multi && data->multi->ssl_seeded)\n    return CURLE_OK;\n\n  if(rand_enough()) {\n    /* OpenSSL 1.1.0+ should return here */\n    if(data->multi)\n      data->multi->ssl_seeded = TRUE;\n    return CURLE_OK;\n  }\n#ifdef HAVE_RANDOM_INIT_BY_DEFAULT\n  /* with OpenSSL 1.1.0+, a failed RAND_status is a showstopper */\n  failf(data, \"Insufficient randomness\");\n  return CURLE_SSL_CONNECT_ERROR;\n#else\n\n  /* fallback to a custom seeding of the PRNG using a hash based on a current\n     time */\n  do {\n    unsigned char randb[64];\n    size_t len = sizeof(randb);\n    size_t i, i_max;\n    for(i = 0, i_max = len / sizeof(struct curltime); i < i_max; ++i) {\n      struct curltime tv = Curl_now();\n      Curl_wait_ms(1);\n      tv.tv_sec *= (time_t)i + 1;\n      tv.tv_usec *= (int)i + 2;\n      tv.tv_sec ^= ((Curl_now().tv_sec + (time_t)Curl_now().tv_usec) *\n                    (time_t)(i + 3)) << 8;\n      tv.tv_usec ^= (int) ((Curl_now().tv_sec + (time_t)Curl_now().tv_usec) *\n                           (time_t)(i + 4)) << 16;\n      memcpy(&randb[i * sizeof(struct curltime)], &tv,\n             sizeof(struct curltime));\n    }\n    RAND_add(randb, (int)len, (double)len/2);\n  } while(!rand_enough());\n\n  {\n    /* generates a default path for the random seed file */\n    char fname[256];\n    fname[0] = 0; /* blank it first */\n    RAND_file_name(fname, sizeof(fname));\n    if(fname[0]) {\n      /* we got a filename to try */\n      RAND_load_file(fname, RAND_LOAD_LENGTH);\n      if(rand_enough())\n        return CURLE_OK;\n    }\n  }\n\n  infof(data, \"libcurl is now using a weak random seed\");\n  return rand_enough() ? CURLE_OK :\n         CURLE_SSL_CONNECT_ERROR; /* confusing error code */\n#endif\n}\n\n#ifndef SSL_FILETYPE_ENGINE\n#define SSL_FILETYPE_ENGINE 42\n#endif\n#ifndef SSL_FILETYPE_PKCS12\n#define SSL_FILETYPE_PKCS12 43\n#endif\n#ifndef SSL_FILETYPE_PROVIDER\n#define SSL_FILETYPE_PROVIDER 44\n#endif\nstatic int ossl_do_file_type(const char *type)\n{\n  if(!type || !type[0])\n    return SSL_FILETYPE_PEM;\n  if(strcasecompare(type, \"PEM\"))\n    return SSL_FILETYPE_PEM;\n  if(strcasecompare(type, \"DER\"))\n    return SSL_FILETYPE_ASN1;\n  if(strcasecompare(type, \"PROV\"))\n    return SSL_FILETYPE_PROVIDER;\n  if(strcasecompare(type, \"ENG\"))\n    return SSL_FILETYPE_ENGINE;\n  if(strcasecompare(type, \"P12\"))\n    return SSL_FILETYPE_PKCS12;\n  return -1;\n}\n\n#if defined(USE_OPENSSL_ENGINE) || defined(OPENSSL_HAS_PROVIDERS)\n/*\n * Supply default password to the engine user interface conversation.\n * The password is passed by OpenSSL engine from ENGINE_load_private_key()\n * last argument to the ui and can be obtained by UI_get0_user_data(ui) here.\n */\nstatic int ssl_ui_reader(UI *ui, UI_STRING *uis)\n{\n  const char *password;\n  switch(UI_get_string_type(uis)) {\n  case UIT_PROMPT:\n  case UIT_VERIFY:\n    password = (const char *)UI_get0_user_data(ui);\n    if(password && (UI_get_input_flags(uis) & UI_INPUT_FLAG_DEFAULT_PWD)) {\n      UI_set_result(ui, uis, password);\n      return 1;\n    }\n    FALLTHROUGH();\n  default:\n    break;\n  }\n  return (UI_method_get_reader(UI_OpenSSL()))(ui, uis);\n}\n\n/*\n * Suppress interactive request for a default password if available.\n */\nstatic int ssl_ui_writer(UI *ui, UI_STRING *uis)\n{\n  switch(UI_get_string_type(uis)) {\n  case UIT_PROMPT:\n  case UIT_VERIFY:\n    if(UI_get0_user_data(ui) &&\n       (UI_get_input_flags(uis) & UI_INPUT_FLAG_DEFAULT_PWD)) {\n      return 1;\n    }\n    FALLTHROUGH();\n  default:\n    break;\n  }\n  return (UI_method_get_writer(UI_OpenSSL()))(ui, uis);\n}\n\n/*\n * Check if a given string is a PKCS#11 URI\n */\nstatic bool is_pkcs11_uri(const char *string)\n{\n  return string && strncasecompare(string, \"pkcs11:\", 7);\n}\n\n#endif\n\nstatic CURLcode ossl_set_engine(struct Curl_easy *data, const char *engine);\n#if !defined(USE_OPENSSL_ENGINE) && defined(OPENSSL_HAS_PROVIDERS)\nstatic CURLcode ossl_set_provider(struct Curl_easy *data,\n                                  const char *provider);\n#endif\n\nstatic int use_certificate_blob(SSL_CTX *ctx, const struct curl_blob *blob,\n                                int type, const char *key_passwd)\n{\n  int ret = 0;\n  X509 *x = NULL;\n  /* the typecast of blob->len is fine since it is guaranteed to never be\n     larger than CURL_MAX_INPUT_LENGTH */\n  BIO *in = BIO_new_mem_buf(blob->data, (int)(blob->len));\n  if(!in)\n    return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n\n  if(type == SSL_FILETYPE_ASN1) {\n    /* j = ERR_R_ASN1_LIB; */\n    x = d2i_X509_bio(in, NULL);\n  }\n  else if(type == SSL_FILETYPE_PEM) {\n    /* ERR_R_PEM_LIB; */\n    x = PEM_read_bio_X509(in, NULL,\n                          passwd_callback, (void *)key_passwd);\n  }\n  else {\n    ret = 0;\n    goto end;\n  }\n\n  if(!x) {\n    ret = 0;\n    goto end;\n  }\n\n  ret = SSL_CTX_use_certificate(ctx, x);\nend:\n  X509_free(x);\n  BIO_free(in);\n  return ret;\n}\n\nstatic int use_privatekey_blob(SSL_CTX *ctx, const struct curl_blob *blob,\n                               int type, const char *key_passwd)\n{\n  int ret = 0;\n  EVP_PKEY *pkey = NULL;\n  BIO *in = BIO_new_mem_buf(blob->data, (int)(blob->len));\n  if(!in)\n    return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n\n  if(type == SSL_FILETYPE_PEM)\n    pkey = PEM_read_bio_PrivateKey(in, NULL, passwd_callback,\n                                   (void *)key_passwd);\n  else if(type == SSL_FILETYPE_ASN1)\n    pkey = d2i_PrivateKey_bio(in, NULL);\n  else\n    goto end;\n\n  if(!pkey)\n    goto end;\n\n  ret = SSL_CTX_use_PrivateKey(ctx, pkey);\n  EVP_PKEY_free(pkey);\nend:\n  BIO_free(in);\n  return ret;\n}\n\nstatic int\nuse_certificate_chain_blob(SSL_CTX *ctx, const struct curl_blob *blob,\n                           const char *key_passwd)\n{\n/* SSL_CTX_add1_chain_cert introduced in OpenSSL 1.0.2 */\n#if (OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x1000200fL) && /* OpenSSL 1.0.2 or later */ \\\n  !(defined(LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER) &&                                     \\\n    (LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER < 0x2090100fL)) /* LibreSSL 2.9.1 or later */\n  int ret = 0;\n  X509 *x = NULL;\n  void *passwd_callback_userdata = (void *)key_passwd;\n  BIO *in = BIO_new_mem_buf(blob->data, (int)(blob->len));\n  if(!in)\n    return CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n\n  ERR_clear_error();\n\n  x = PEM_read_bio_X509_AUX(in, NULL,\n                            passwd_callback, (void *)key_passwd);\n  if(!x)\n    goto end;\n\n  ret = SSL_CTX_use_certificate(ctx, x);\n\n  if(ERR_peek_error() != 0)\n    ret = 0;\n\n  if(ret) {\n    X509 *ca;\n    sslerr_t err;\n\n    if(!SSL_CTX_clear_chain_certs(ctx)) {\n      ret = 0;\n      goto end;\n    }\n\n    while((ca = PEM_read_bio_X509(in, NULL, passwd_callback,\n                                  passwd_callback_userdata))\n          != NULL) {\n\n      if(!SSL_CTX_add0_chain_cert(ctx, ca)) {\n        X509_free(ca);\n        ret = 0;\n        goto end;\n      }\n    }\n\n    err = ERR_peek_last_error();\n    if((ERR_GET_LIB(err) == ERR_LIB_PEM) &&\n       (ERR_GET_REASON(err) == PEM_R_NO_START_LINE))\n      ERR_clear_error();\n    else\n      ret = 0;\n  }\n\nend:\n  X509_free(x);\n  BIO_free(in);\n  return ret;\n#else\n  (void)ctx; /* unused */\n  (void)blob; /* unused */\n  (void)key_passwd; /* unused */\n  return 0;\n#endif\n}\n\nstatic\nint cert_stuff(struct Curl_easy *data,\n               SSL_CTX* ctx,\n               char *cert_file,\n               const struct curl_blob *cert_blob,\n               const char *cert_type,\n               char *key_file,\n               const struct curl_blob *key_blob,\n               const char *key_type,\n               char *key_passwd)\n{\n  char error_buffer[256];\n  bool check_privkey = TRUE;\n\n  int file_type = ossl_do_file_type(cert_type);\n\n  if(cert_file || cert_blob || (file_type == SSL_FILETYPE_ENGINE) ||\n     (file_type == SSL_FILETYPE_PROVIDER)) {\n    SSL *ssl;\n    X509 *x509;\n    int cert_done = 0;\n    int cert_use_result;\n\n    if(key_passwd) {\n      /* set the password in the callback userdata */\n      SSL_CTX_set_default_passwd_cb_userdata(ctx, key_passwd);\n      /* Set passwd callback: */\n      SSL_CTX_set_default_passwd_cb(ctx, passwd_callback);\n    }\n\n\n    switch(file_type) {\n    case SSL_FILETYPE_PEM:\n      /* SSL_CTX_use_certificate_chain_file() only works on PEM files */\n      cert_use_result = cert_blob ?\n        use_certificate_chain_blob(ctx, cert_blob, key_passwd) :\n        SSL_CTX_use_certificate_chain_file(ctx, cert_file);\n      if(cert_use_result != 1) {\n        failf(data,\n              \"could not load PEM client certificate from %s, \" OSSL_PACKAGE\n              \" error %s, \"\n              \"(no key found, wrong pass phrase, or wrong file format?)\",\n              (cert_blob ? \"CURLOPT_SSLCERT_BLOB\" : cert_file),\n              ossl_strerror(ERR_get_error(), error_buffer,\n                            sizeof(error_buffer)) );\n        return 0;\n      }\n      break;\n\n    case SSL_FILETYPE_ASN1:\n      /* SSL_CTX_use_certificate_file() works with either PEM or ASN1, but\n         we use the case above for PEM so this can only be performed with\n         ASN1 files. */\n\n      cert_use_result = cert_blob ?\n        use_certificate_blob(ctx, cert_blob, file_type, key_passwd) :\n      SSL_CTX_use_certificate_file(ctx, cert_file, file_type);\n      if(cert_use_result != 1) {\n        failf(data,\n              \"could not load ASN1 client certificate from %s, \" OSSL_PACKAGE\n              \" error %s, \"\n              \"(no key found, wrong pass phrase, or wrong file format?)\",\n              (cert_blob ? \"CURLOPT_SSLCERT_BLOB\" : cert_file),\n              ossl_strerror(ERR_get_error(), error_buffer,\n                            sizeof(error_buffer)) );\n        return 0;\n      }\n      break;\n    case SSL_FILETYPE_ENGINE:\n#if defined(USE_OPENSSL_ENGINE) && defined(ENGINE_CTRL_GET_CMD_FROM_NAME)\n    {\n      /* Implicitly use pkcs11 engine if none was provided and the\n       * cert_file is a PKCS#11 URI */\n      if(!data->state.engine) {\n        if(is_pkcs11_uri(cert_file)) {\n          if(ossl_set_engine(data, \"pkcs11\") != CURLE_OK) {\n            return 0;\n          }\n        }\n      }\n\n      if(data->state.engine) {\n        const char *cmd_name = \"LOAD_CERT_CTRL\";\n        struct {\n          const char *cert_id;\n          X509 *cert;\n        } params;\n\n        params.cert_id = cert_file;\n        params.cert = NULL;\n\n        /* Does the engine supports LOAD_CERT_CTRL ? */\n        if(!ENGINE_ctrl(data->state.engine, ENGINE_CTRL_GET_CMD_FROM_NAME,\n                        0, (void *)cmd_name, NULL)) {\n          failf(data, \"ssl engine does not support loading certificates\");\n          return 0;\n        }\n\n        /* Load the certificate from the engine */\n        if(!ENGINE_ctrl_cmd(data->state.engine, cmd_name,\n                            0, &params, NULL, 1)) {\n          failf(data, \"ssl engine cannot load client cert with id\"\n                \" '%s' [%s]\", cert_file,\n                ossl_strerror(ERR_get_error(), error_buffer,\n                              sizeof(error_buffer)));\n          return 0;\n        }\n\n        if(!params.cert) {\n          failf(data, \"ssl engine did not initialized the certificate \"\n                \"properly.\");\n          return 0;\n        }\n\n        if(SSL_CTX_use_certificate(ctx, params.cert) != 1) {\n          failf(data, \"unable to set client certificate [%s]\",\n                ossl_strerror(ERR_get_error(), error_buffer,\n                              sizeof(error_buffer)));\n          return 0;\n        }\n        X509_free(params.cert); /* we do not need the handle any more... */\n      }\n      else {\n        failf(data, \"crypto engine not set, cannot load certificate\");\n        return 0;\n      }\n    }\n    break;\n#elif defined(OPENSSL_HAS_PROVIDERS)\n      /* fall through to compatible provider */\n    case SSL_FILETYPE_PROVIDER:\n    {\n      /* Implicitly use pkcs11 provider if none was provided and the\n       * cert_file is a PKCS#11 URI */\n      if(!data->state.provider) {\n        if(is_pkcs11_uri(cert_file)) {\n          if(ossl_set_provider(data, \"pkcs11\") != CURLE_OK) {\n            return 0;\n          }\n        }\n      }\n\n      if(data->state.provider) {\n        /* Load the certificate from the provider */\n        OSSL_STORE_CTX *store = NULL;\n        OSSL_STORE_INFO *info = NULL;\n        X509 *cert = NULL;\n        store = OSSL_STORE_open(cert_file, NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL);\n        if(!store) {\n          failf(data, \"Failed to open OpenSSL store: %s\",\n                ossl_strerror(ERR_get_error(), error_buffer,\n                              sizeof(error_buffer)));\n          return 0;\n        }\n        if(OSSL_STORE_expect(store, OSSL_STORE_INFO_CERT) != 1) {\n          failf(data, \"Failed to set store preference. Ignoring the error: %s\",\n                ossl_strerror(ERR_get_error(), error_buffer,\n                              sizeof(error_buffer)));\n        }\n\n        for(info = OSSL_STORE_load(store);\n            info != NULL;\n            info = OSSL_STORE_load(store)) {\n          int ossl_type = OSSL_STORE_INFO_get_type(info);\n\n          if(ossl_type == OSSL_STORE_INFO_CERT) {\n            cert = OSSL_STORE_INFO_get1_CERT(info);\n          }\n          else {\n            failf(data, \"Ignoring object not matching our type: %d\",\n                  ossl_type);\n            OSSL_STORE_INFO_free(info);\n            continue;\n          }\n          OSSL_STORE_INFO_free(info);\n          break;\n        }\n        OSSL_STORE_close(store);\n        if(!cert) {\n          failf(data, \"No cert found in the openssl store: %s\",\n                ossl_strerror(ERR_get_error(), error_buffer,\n                              sizeof(error_buffer)));\n          goto fail;\n        }\n\n        if(SSL_CTX_use_certificate(ctx, cert) != 1) {\n          failf(data, \"unable to set client certificate [%s]\",\n                ossl_strerror(ERR_get_error(), error_buffer,\n                              sizeof(error_buffer)));\n          return 0;\n        }\n        X509_free(cert); /* we do not need the handle any more... */\n      }\n      else {\n        failf(data, \"crypto provider not set, cannot load certificate\");\n        return 0;\n      }\n    }\n    break;\n#else\n    failf(data, \"file type ENG nor PROV for certificate not implemented\");\n    return 0;\n#endif\n\n    case SSL_FILETYPE_PKCS12:\n    {\n      BIO *cert_bio = NULL;\n      PKCS12 *p12 = NULL;\n      EVP_PKEY *pri;\n      STACK_OF(X509) *ca = NULL;\n      if(cert_blob) {\n        cert_bio = BIO_new_mem_buf(cert_blob->data, (int)(cert_blob->len));\n        if(!cert_bio) {\n          failf(data,\n                \"BIO_new_mem_buf NULL, \" OSSL_PACKAGE\n                \" error %s\",\n                ossl_strerror(ERR_get_error(), error_buffer,\n                              sizeof(error_buffer)) );\n          return 0;\n        }\n      }\n      else {\n        cert_bio = BIO_new(BIO_s_file());\n        if(!cert_bio) {\n          failf(data,\n                \"BIO_new return NULL, \" OSSL_PACKAGE\n                \" error %s\",\n                ossl_strerror(ERR_get_error(), error_buffer,\n                              sizeof(error_buffer)) );\n          return 0;\n        }\n\n        if(BIO_read_filename(cert_bio, cert_file) <= 0) {\n          failf(data, \"could not open PKCS12 file '%s'\", cert_file);\n          BIO_free(cert_bio);\n          return 0;\n        }\n      }\n\n      p12 = d2i_PKCS12_bio(cert_bio, NULL);\n      BIO_free(cert_bio);\n\n      if(!p12) {\n        failf(data, \"error reading PKCS12 file '%s'\",\n              cert_blob ? \"(memory blob)\" : cert_file);\n        return 0;\n      }\n\n      PKCS12_PBE_add();\n\n      if(!PKCS12_parse(p12, key_passwd, &pri, &x509,\n                       &ca)) {\n        failf(data,\n              \"could not parse PKCS12 file, check password, \" OSSL_PACKAGE\n              \" error %s\",\n              ossl_strerror(ERR_get_error(), error_buffer,\n                            sizeof(error_buffer)) );\n        PKCS12_free(p12);\n        return 0;\n      }\n\n      PKCS12_free(p12);\n\n      if(SSL_CTX_use_certificate(ctx, x509) != 1) {\n        failf(data,\n              \"could not load PKCS12 client certificate, \" OSSL_PACKAGE\n              \" error %s\",\n              ossl_strerror(ERR_get_error(), error_buffer,\n                            sizeof(error_buffer)) );\n        goto fail;\n      }\n\n      if(SSL_CTX_use_PrivateKey(ctx, pri) != 1) {\n        failf(data, \"unable to use private key from PKCS12 file '%s'\",\n              cert_file);\n        goto fail;\n      }\n\n      if(!SSL_CTX_check_private_key (ctx)) {\n        failf(data, \"private key from PKCS12 file '%s' \"\n              \"does not match certificate in same file\", cert_file);\n        goto fail;\n      }\n      /* Set Certificate Verification chain */\n      if(ca) {\n        while(sk_X509_num(ca)) {\n          /*\n           * Note that sk_X509_pop() is used below to make sure the cert is\n           * removed from the stack properly before getting passed to\n           * SSL_CTX_add_extra_chain_cert(), which takes ownership. Previously\n           * we used sk_X509_value() instead, but then we would clean it in the\n           * subsequent sk_X509_pop_free() call.\n           */\n          X509 *x = sk_X509_pop(ca);\n          if(!SSL_CTX_add_client_CA(ctx, x)) {\n            X509_free(x);\n            failf(data, \"cannot add certificate to client CA list\");\n            goto fail;\n          }\n          if(!SSL_CTX_add_extra_chain_cert(ctx, x)) {\n            X509_free(x);\n            failf(data, \"cannot add certificate to certificate chain\");\n            goto fail;\n          }\n        }\n      }\n\n      cert_done = 1;\nfail:\n      EVP_PKEY_free(pri);\n      X509_free(x509);\n      sk_X509_pop_free(ca, X509_free);\n      if(!cert_done)\n        return 0; /* failure! */\n      break;\n    }\n    default:\n      failf(data, \"not supported file type '%s' for certificate\", cert_type);\n      return 0;\n    }\n\n    if((!key_file) && (!key_blob)) {\n      key_file = cert_file;\n      key_blob = cert_blob;\n    }\n    else\n      file_type = ossl_do_file_type(key_type);\n\n    switch(file_type) {\n    case SSL_FILETYPE_PEM:\n      if(cert_done)\n        break;\n      FALLTHROUGH();\n    case SSL_FILETYPE_ASN1:\n      cert_use_result = key_blob ?\n        use_privatekey_blob(ctx, key_blob, file_type, key_passwd) :\n      SSL_CTX_use_PrivateKey_file(ctx, key_file, file_type);\n      if(cert_use_result != 1) {\n        failf(data, \"unable to set private key file: '%s' type %s\",\n              key_file ? key_file : \"(memory blob)\",\n              key_type ? key_type : \"PEM\");\n        return 0;\n      }\n      break;\n    case SSL_FILETYPE_ENGINE:\n#ifdef USE_OPENSSL_ENGINE\n    {\n      EVP_PKEY *priv_key = NULL;\n\n      /* Implicitly use pkcs11 engine if none was provided and the\n       * key_file is a PKCS#11 URI */\n      if(!data->state.engine) {\n        if(is_pkcs11_uri(key_file)) {\n          if(ossl_set_engine(data, \"pkcs11\") != CURLE_OK) {\n            return 0;\n          }\n        }\n      }\n\n      if(data->state.engine) {\n        UI_METHOD *ui_method =\n          UI_create_method((char *)\"curl user interface\");\n        if(!ui_method) {\n          failf(data, \"unable do create \" OSSL_PACKAGE\n                \" user-interface method\");\n          return 0;\n        }\n        UI_method_set_opener(ui_method, UI_method_get_opener(UI_OpenSSL()));\n        UI_method_set_closer(ui_method, UI_method_get_closer(UI_OpenSSL()));\n        UI_method_set_reader(ui_method, ssl_ui_reader);\n        UI_method_set_writer(ui_method, ssl_ui_writer);\n        priv_key = ENGINE_load_private_key(data->state.engine, key_file,\n                                           ui_method,\n                                           key_passwd);\n        UI_destroy_method(ui_method);\n        if(!priv_key) {\n          failf(data, \"failed to load private key from crypto engine\");\n          return 0;\n        }\n        if(SSL_CTX_use_PrivateKey(ctx, priv_key) != 1) {\n          failf(data, \"unable to set private key\");\n          EVP_PKEY_free(priv_key);\n          return 0;\n        }\n        EVP_PKEY_free(priv_key);  /* we do not need the handle any more... */\n      }\n      else {\n        failf(data, \"crypto engine not set, cannot load private key\");\n        return 0;\n      }\n    }\n    break;\n#elif defined(OPENSSL_HAS_PROVIDERS)\n      /* fall through to compatible provider */\n    case SSL_FILETYPE_PROVIDER:\n    {\n      /* Implicitly use pkcs11 provider if none was provided and the\n       * cert_file is a PKCS#11 URI */\n      if(!data->state.provider) {\n        if(is_pkcs11_uri(cert_file)) {\n          if(ossl_set_provider(data, \"pkcs11\") != CURLE_OK) {\n            return 0;\n          }\n        }\n      }\n\n      if(data->state.provider) {\n        /* Load the private key from the provider */\n        EVP_PKEY *priv_key = NULL;\n        OSSL_STORE_CTX *store = NULL;\n        OSSL_STORE_INFO *info = NULL;\n        UI_METHOD *ui_method =\n          UI_create_method((char *)\"curl user interface\");\n        if(!ui_method) {\n          failf(data, \"unable do create \" OSSL_PACKAGE\n                \" user-interface method\");\n          return 0;\n        }\n        UI_method_set_opener(ui_method, UI_method_get_opener(UI_OpenSSL()));\n        UI_method_set_closer(ui_method, UI_method_get_closer(UI_OpenSSL()));\n        UI_method_set_reader(ui_method, ssl_ui_reader);\n        UI_method_set_writer(ui_method, ssl_ui_writer);\n\n        store = OSSL_STORE_open(key_file, ui_method, NULL, NULL, NULL);\n        if(!store) {\n          failf(data, \"Failed to open OpenSSL store: %s\",\n                ossl_strerror(ERR_get_error(), error_buffer,\n                              sizeof(error_buffer)));\n          return 0;\n        }\n        if(OSSL_STORE_expect(store, OSSL_STORE_INFO_PKEY) != 1) {\n          failf(data, \"Failed to set store preference. Ignoring the error: %s\",\n                ossl_strerror(ERR_get_error(), error_buffer,\n                              sizeof(error_buffer)));\n        }\n\n        for(info = OSSL_STORE_load(store);\n            info != NULL;\n            info = OSSL_STORE_load(store)) {\n          int ossl_type = OSSL_STORE_INFO_get_type(info);\n\n          if(ossl_type == OSSL_STORE_INFO_PKEY) {\n            priv_key = OSSL_STORE_INFO_get1_PKEY(info);\n          }\n          else {\n            failf(data, \"Ignoring object not matching our type: %d\",\n                  ossl_type);\n            OSSL_STORE_INFO_free(info);\n            continue;\n          }\n          OSSL_STORE_INFO_free(info);\n          break;\n        }\n        OSSL_STORE_close(store);\n        UI_destroy_method(ui_method);\n        if(!priv_key) {\n          failf(data, \"No private key found in the openssl store: %s\",\n                ossl_strerror(ERR_get_error(), error_buffer,\n                              sizeof(error_buffer)));\n          goto fail;\n        }\n\n        if(SSL_CTX_use_PrivateKey(ctx, priv_key) != 1) {\n          failf(data, \"unable to set private key [%s]\",\n                ossl_strerror(ERR_get_error(), error_buffer,\n                              sizeof(error_buffer)));\n          EVP_PKEY_free(priv_key);\n          return 0;\n        }\n        EVP_PKEY_free(priv_key); /* we do not need the handle any more... */\n      }\n      else {\n        failf(data, \"crypto provider not set, cannot load private key\");\n        return 0;\n      }\n    }\n    break;\n#else\n    failf(data, \"file type ENG nor PROV for private key not implemented\");\n    return 0;\n#endif\n\n    case SSL_FILETYPE_PKCS12:\n      if(!cert_done) {\n        failf(data, \"file type P12 for private key not supported\");\n        return 0;\n      }\n      break;\n    default:\n      failf(data, \"not supported file type for private key\");\n      return 0;\n    }\n\n    ssl = SSL_new(ctx);\n    if(!ssl) {\n      failf(data, \"unable to create an SSL structure\");\n      return 0;\n    }\n\n    x509 = SSL_get_certificate(ssl);\n\n    /* This version was provided by Evan Jordan and is supposed to not\n       leak memory as the previous version: */\n    if(x509) {\n      EVP_PKEY *pktmp = X509_get_pubkey(x509);\n      EVP_PKEY_copy_parameters(pktmp, SSL_get_privatekey(ssl));\n      EVP_PKEY_free(pktmp);\n    }\n\n#if !defined(OPENSSL_NO_RSA) && !defined(OPENSSL_IS_BORINGSSL) &&       \\\n  !defined(OPENSSL_NO_DEPRECATED_3_0)\n    {\n      /* If RSA is used, do not check the private key if its flags indicate\n       * it does not support it. */\n      EVP_PKEY *priv_key = SSL_get_privatekey(ssl);\n      int pktype;\n#ifdef HAVE_OPAQUE_EVP_PKEY\n      pktype = EVP_PKEY_id(priv_key);\n#else\n      pktype = priv_key->type;\n#endif\n      if(pktype == EVP_PKEY_RSA) {\n        RSA *rsa = EVP_PKEY_get1_RSA(priv_key);\n        if(RSA_flags(rsa) & RSA_METHOD_FLAG_NO_CHECK)\n          check_privkey = FALSE;\n        RSA_free(rsa); /* Decrement reference count */\n      }\n    }\n#endif\n\n    SSL_free(ssl);\n\n    /* If we are using DSA, we can copy the parameters from\n     * the private key */\n\n    if(check_privkey == TRUE) {\n      /* Now we know that a key and cert have been set against\n       * the SSL context */\n      if(!SSL_CTX_check_private_key(ctx)) {\n        failf(data, \"Private key does not match the certificate public key\");\n        return 0;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n  return 1;\n}\n\n/* returns non-zero on failure */\nstatic CURLcode x509_name_oneline(X509_NAME *a, struct dynbuf *d)\n{\n  BIO *bio_out = BIO_new(BIO_s_mem());\n  BUF_MEM *biomem;\n  int rc;\n  CURLcode result = CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\n\n  if(bio_out) {\n    Curl_dyn_reset(d);\n    rc = X509_NAME_print_ex(bio_out, a, 0, XN_FLAG_SEP_SPLUS_SPC);\n    if(rc != -1) {\n      BIO_get_mem_ptr(bio_out, &biomem);\n      result = Curl_dyn_addn(d, biomem->data, biomem->length);\n      BIO_free(bio_out);\n    }\n  }\n  return result;\n}\n\n/**\n * Global SSL init\n *\n * @retval 0 error initializing SSL\n * @retval 1 SSL initialized successfully\n */\nstatic int ossl_init(void)\n{\n#if (OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x10100000L) &&  \\\n  (!defined(LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER) || LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x2070000fL)\n  const uint64_t flags =\n#ifdef OPENSSL_INIT_ENGINE_ALL_BUILTIN\n    /* not present in BoringSSL */\n    OPENSSL_INIT_ENGINE_ALL_BUILTIN |\n#endif\n#ifdef CURL_DISABLE_OPENSSL_AUTO_LOAD_CONFIG\n    OPENSSL_INIT_NO_LOAD_CONFIG |\n#else\n    OPENSSL_INIT_LOAD_CONFIG |\n#endif\n    0;\n  OPENSSL_init_ssl(flags, NULL);\n#else\n  OPENSSL_load_builtin_modules();\n\n#ifdef USE_OPENSSL_ENGINE\n  ENGINE_load_builtin_engines();\n#endif\n\n/* CONF_MFLAGS_DEFAULT_SECTION was introduced some time between 0.9.8b and\n   0.9.8e */\n#ifndef CONF_MFLAGS_DEFAULT_SECTION\n#define CONF_MFLAGS_DEFAULT_SECTION 0x0\n#endif\n\n#ifndef CURL_DISABLE_OPENSSL_AUTO_LOAD_CONFIG\n  CONF_modules_load_file(NULL, NULL,\n                         CONF_MFLAGS_DEFAULT_SECTION|\n                         CONF_MFLAGS_IGNORE_MISSING_FILE);\n#endif\n\n  /* Let's get nice error messages */\n  SSL_load_error_strings();\n\n  /* Init the global ciphers and digests */\n  if(!SSLeay_add_ssl_algorithms())\n    return 0;\n\n  OpenSSL_add_all_algorithms();\n#endif\n\n  Curl_tls_keylog_open();\n\n  return 1;\n}\n\n/* Global cleanup */\nstatic void ossl_cleanup(void)\n{\n#if (OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x10100000L) &&  \\\n  (!defined(LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER) || LIBRESSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x2070000fL)\n  /* OpenSSL 1.1 deprecates all these cleanup functions and\n     turns them into no-ops in OpenSSL 1.0 compatibility mode */\n#else\n  /* Free ciphers and digests lists */\n  EVP_cleanup();\n\n#ifdef USE_OPENSSL_ENGINE\n  /* Free engine list */\n  ENGINE_cleanup();\n#endif\n\n  /* Free OpenSSL error strings */\n  ERR_free_strings();\n\n  /* Free thread local error state, destroying hash upon zero refcount */\n#ifdef HAVE_ERR_REMOVE_THREAD_STATE\n  ERR_remove_thread_state(NULL);\n#else\n  ERR_remove_state(0);\n#endif\n\n  /* Free all memory allocated by all configuration modules */\n  CONF_modules_free();\n\n#ifdef HAVE_SSL_COMP_FREE_COMPRESSION_METHODS\n  SSL_COMP_free_compression_methods();\n#endif\n#endif\n\n  Curl_tls_keylog_close();\n}\n\n/* Selects an OpenSSL crypto engine\n */\nstatic CURLcode ossl_set_engine(struct Curl_easy *data, const char *engine)\n{\n#ifdef USE_OPENSSL_ENGINE\n  ENGINE *e;\n\n#if OPENSSL_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x00909000L\n  e = ENGINE_by_id(engine);\n#else\n  /* avoid memory leak */\n  for(e = ENGINE_get_first(); e; e = ENGINE_get_next(e)) {\n    const char *e_id = ENGINE_get_id(e);\n    if(!strcmp(engine, e_id))\n      break;\n  }\n#endif\n\n  if(!e) {\n    failf(data, \"SSL Engine '%s' not found\", engine);\n    return CURLE_SSL_ENGINE_NOTFOUND;\n  }\n\n  if(data->state.engine) {\n    ENGINE_finish(data->state.engine);\n    ENGINE_free(data->state.engine);\n    data->state.engine = NULL;\n  }\n  if(!ENGINE_init(e)) {\n    char buf[256];\n\n    ENGINE_free(e);\n    failf(data, \"Failed to initialise SSL Engine '%s': %s\",\n          engine, ossl_strerror(ERR_get_error(), buf, sizeof(buf)));\n    return CURLE_SSL_ENGINE_INITFAILED;\n  }\n  data->state.engine = e;\n  return CURLE_OK;\n#else\n  (void)engine;\n  failf(data, \"SSL Engine not supported\");\n  return CURLE_SSL_ENGINE_NOTFOUND;\n#endif\n}\n\n/* Sets engine as default for all SSL operations\n */\nstatic CURLcode ossl_set_engine_default(struct Curl_easy *data)\n{\n#ifdef USE_OPENSSL_ENGINE\n  if(data->state.engine) {\n    if(ENGINE_set_default(data->state.engine, ENGINE_METHOD_ALL) > 0) {\n
